items.titleEn: Key takeaways: Rays 7, Blue Jays 1
bodyEn: TORONTO -- Day by day, you can feel the pressure building around and amongst the Blue Jays.

The 100th game of the season brought another loss, this time a 7-1 defeat by the Rays, and the Blue Jays don’t exactly have 62 games to work with here. The Trade Deadline looms just 13 games away on Aug. 3, and the Blue Jays are tilting themselves in the wrong direction at 46-54.
This is what mattered most from Monday’s loss:
1. Frustrations are building …

Soon, there may come a day when bats are broken and dugouts are redecorated in fits of rage. For now, we’re left with the early signs of frustration from the Blue Jays, a team staring down their own mortality.

Vladimir Guerrero Jr.’s at-bat in the third inning captured this so well. Guerrero turned on the first pitch he saw and launched it to left field, for what finally looked like his first home run at Rogers Centre this season. Guerrero didn’t get all of it, though, and when it fell short at the base of the wall into Chandler Simpson’s glove, there was a feeling of … “of course.”

Guerrero pulled his helmet off near first base and held it above his head with both hands, wanting so badly to break it in half on the ground below him, but he held off. If things keep trending in this direction for the Blue Jays, the next helmet, bat or bucket of sunflower seeds in the dugout may not be given such mercy.
2. How do struggling teams manage pressure?

If this were the NHL, John Schneider could walk into the clubhouse tomorrow and tell his players to try a little harder. Play physically in the first few shifts, maybe drop the gloves for a scrap to fire up the team. It doesn’t work that way in baseball.

Giving 110% can backfire, and it’s so often the challenge for struggling teams. The Blue Jays are desperate to hit for more power, but when you start chasing it, that’s a one-way ticket to getting fooled by a changeup and ending up on one knee for strike three.

How, then, do the Blue Jays play with urgency without overdoing it? In baseball, you need to find the right version of your 100%, which the Blue Jays clearly haven’t found thus far. Where’s the sweet spot?

“You don’t want guys to play tense,” Schneider said prior to Monday’s game. “There’s a back and forth. This is a big four-game series, but it’s a one-game series today. That’s what I told them. I told them today in the hitters meeting that if we lose today, we’re going to play tomorrow. We have a roof. It’s not going to be rained out. We’re going to play. You have to focus on today and figure out what’s important.”
3. Salt in the wound: Losing the Cease game
Looking at this four-game set -- or any series that Dylan Cease pitches in -- he’s the Blue Jays’ best shot. To lose Cease’s game right out of the gates only adds to the frustration from Monday.

Cease allowed five runs (three earned) over six innings, including a pair of home runs. He struck out seven, but the Rays made enough loud contact to beat the Cy Young Award candidate, who now owns a 2.67 ERA with 155 strikeouts over 104 1/3 innings. Cease still needs to reel in New York’s Cam Schlittler, but his best shot at the Cy is putting up a strikeout total that makes voters say “wow.”

With a dozen starts left in his season, the Blue Jays need to be cashing in on days their ace pitches. If Cease stumbles even a little bit, though, this offense isn’t doing enough to save him or the rest of his teammates in the Blue Jays’ rotation.
items.titleZh: 重点回顾：光芒 7-1 蓝鸟
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟1-7不敌光芒，压力持续累积。
bodyZh: 多伦多——日复一日，你能感受到蓝鸟周围和内部积聚的压力。
赛季第100场比赛带来了又一场失利，这次是1-7输给光芒，而蓝鸟并没有62场比赛来调整。交易截止日仅剩13场比赛，即8月3日，而蓝鸟以46胜54负的战绩正朝着错误的方向倾斜。
以下是周一失利中最重要的事情：
1. 挫败感正在累积……
很快，也许会出现球棒折断、休息室被愤怒重新装饰的一天。目前，我们只看到了蓝鸟球员早期挫败感的迹象，这支球队正在面对自己的终结。
Vladimir Guerrero Jr. 在第三局的打席完美地体现了这一点。Guerrero 挥击他看到的第一个球，将球击向左外野，看起来终于要迎来他本赛季在罗杰斯中心的首支本垒打。然而，Guerrero 并没有完全击中，球在墙基处落入 Chandler Simpson 的手套时，有一种“果然如此”的感觉。
Guerrero 在一垒附近摘下头盔，双手举过头顶，非常想把它摔在地上摔成两半，但他忍住了。如果蓝鸟的情况继续这样发展下去，下一个头盔、球棒或休息室里的那桶瓜子可能不会得到如此仁慈的对待。
2. 挣扎的球队如何应对压力？
如果这是 NHL，John Schneider 明天可以走进更衣室告诉球员们更努力一点。在前几次换人时打得有侵略性，也许可以扔掉手套干一架来激励球队。但在棒球中不是这样。
付出110%的努力可能会适得其反，这常常是挣扎球队面临的挑战。蓝鸟迫切希望打出更多力量，但当你开始追逐它时，那是一条被变速球欺骗、最后单膝跪地三振出局的不归路。
那么，蓝鸟如何在不做过头的情况下带着紧迫感比赛？在棒球中，你需要找到自己100%的正确版本，而蓝鸟显然至今还没找到。最佳点在哪里？
"你不想让球员们打得紧张，" Schneider 在周一比赛前说道。"这是来回的。这是一个重要的四场系列赛，但今天是一场系列赛。这就是我告诉他们的。我今天在打者会议上告诉他们，如果我们今天输了，我们明天还会比赛。我们有屋顶。不会因为下雨而取消。我们会比赛。你必须专注于今天，弄清楚什么才是重要的。"
3. 雪上加霜：输掉 Cease 的比赛
纵观这个四场系列赛——或者说任何 Dylan Cease 先发的系列赛——他是蓝鸟最好的机会。一上来就输掉 Cease 的比赛只会增加周一的挫败感。
Cease 在六局中失掉五分（三分自责），包括两支本垒打。他投出七次三振，但光芒队制造了足够多的强劲击球，击败了这位赛扬奖候选人。Cease 现在拥有2.67的自责分率和155次三振，投了104 1/3局。Cease 仍然需要追上纽约的 Cam Schlittler，但他争夺赛扬奖的最佳机会是投出一个让投票者惊叹的三振总数。
在赛季还剩大约12次先发的情况下，蓝鸟需要在他们王牌投球的日子里取得胜利。但如果 Cease 哪怕有一点点失误，这支进攻线也不足以拯救他或蓝鸟轮换中的其他队友。

items.titleEn: Yesavage's 9-K start a bright spot on another quiet day for Blue Jays' bats
bodyEn: TORONTO -- The Blue Jays wanted to come out of the All-Star break looking like a new team, but this series loss to the White Sox carried over all of the old themes from the first half.
Sunday afternoon’s 3-0 loss left the Blue Jays (46-53) still scrambling to gain some traction in an American League Wild Card race that won’t wait around for them forever.
This is what mattered most Sunday:
1. Trey Yesavage? Better.

Last time out, Yesavage walked seven batters in San Diego, a worrying trend that’s popped up a few times this season. Sunday’s performance looked so much closer to the normal version of Yesavage, but unfortunately, the Blue Jays’ bats needed him to be perfect.

Yesavage allowed three runs over six innings of work, including a home run to Colson Montgomery, but he struck out nine and was missing bats all afternoon, a very encouraging sign after some of his recent outings.

At this point, Toronto needs to steal games. We’ve seen 99 games of a team that’s stuck hovering just below .500. Unless a half-dozen things all start to go right at the same time, which feels like a tall ask, the Blue Jays need their star players to turn a few losses into wins along the way with game-changing performances. Yesavage couldn’t do that Sunday, given so little help from the offense, but he’s absolutely capable of doing it if he keeps pitching like this.
2. Another very quiet day for the bats
Two days, one run. That won’t get the Blue Jays very far.

It’s up to this lineup to convince the front office that the Blue Jays should still be buyers ahead of the Aug. 3 Trade Deadline, not just another team stuck in the middle. It doesn’t feel like this team will suddenly shift into an identity of stringing together hits and moving runners, so the obvious pathway to salvaging this is power.

That’s exactly how the White Sox have become one of the best stories in baseball this season, fourth in MLB with 131 home runs and threats throughout their lineup. The Blue Jays are tied for 22nd with 101 and rank 26th in slugging percentage (.382). If it weren’t for Kazuma Okamoto and his 22 home runs, we may already be talking about the Blue Jays as sellers.
3. Still a steep hill to climb

If you’re flicking through the channels or MLB.TV at night, it’s time to start keeping an eye on the mess of teams around the Blue Jays. That means the Tigers, Astros, Orioles, Twins, Mariners, Red Sox, White Sox, Rangers and Guardians. It might help to write these all down.

Not only do the Blue Jays need to play better baseball, they need some nights where most of those teams lose. This is so crowded that playing “just a bit better” won’t cut it. The Blue Jays need some help, then to go on a legitimate run of their own.

Next up? The Rays, who come to town Monday for a four-game set. You’ll be forgiven if those words do not fill you with hope, as the AL-leading Rays have always been hard on the Blue Jays and have beaten them in five of six this season. Surviving with a series split would be good enough, frankly, but the Blue Jays would need to go on a long series winning streak following that. Dropping three of four -- or taking a sweep to the Rays -- could quickly change the conversations we’re having about the Blue Jays and the Trade Deadline.
items.titleZh: Yesavage九次三振先发成蓝鸟打线沉寂中的亮点
items.summaryZh: Yesavage送出9次三振，但蓝鸟打线两日仅得1分，系列赛不敌白袜。
bodyZh: 多伦多 -- 蓝鸟本想在全明星赛后以全新面貌出现，但本次系列赛输给白袜，延续了上半季的所有老问题。
周日下午的0比3失利让蓝鸟（46胜53负）仍在美联外卡争夺中挣扎，而竞争不会永远等他们。
以下是周日最关键的内容：
1. Trey Yesavage？状态更好。
上一次出赛，Yesavage在圣迭戈投出七次保送，这是本赛季出现多次的令人担忧的趋势。周日的表现则更接近Yesavage的正常水平，但不幸的是，蓝鸟打线需要他完美发挥。
Yesavage先发六局失三分，包括被Colson Montgomery击出本垒打，但他送出九次三振，整个下午都在让打者挥空，这是近期出赛后非常令人鼓舞的信号。
眼下，多伦多需要偷取胜利。我们已经看到了99场比赛，球队一直在五成胜率以下徘徊。除非五六件事同时好转——这似乎要求过高——蓝鸟需要他们的明星球员用改变比赛的表现将一些失利转化为胜利。Yesavage周日无法做到，因为进攻端支持太少，但如果他继续这样投球，他绝对有能力做到。
2. 打线又一个非常安静的一天
两天时间，仅得一分。这不会让蓝鸟走得太远。
这个打线需要说服管理层，蓝鸟在8月3日交易截止日前仍应该是买家，而不是另一支中游球队。感觉这支球队不会突然转变为连续安打和推进跑者的风格，因此拯救局面的明显途径是力量。
这正是白袜本赛季成为棒球界最佳故事之一的方式——以131支本垒打位列全联盟第四，打线上下都有威胁。蓝鸟以101支本垒打并列第22位，长打率（.382）排第26。如果不是Kazuma Okamoto和他的22支本垒打，我们可能已经在讨论蓝鸟作为卖家了。
3. 仍有陡峭的山坡要爬
如果你晚上在切换频道或看MLB.TV，是时候开始关注蓝鸟周围的一堆球队了。这意味着老虎、太空人、金莺、双城、水手、红袜、白袜、游骑兵和守护者。或许把这些都写下来会有帮助。
蓝鸟不仅需要打出更好的棒球，还需要有些晚上上述大多数球队都输球。竞争如此激烈，仅仅“表现好一点”是不够的。蓝鸟需要一些帮助，然后自己也要真正地连胜。
下一场？光芒队，他们周一来到主场进行四场系列赛。如果这句话没有让你充满希望，那也可以理解，因为美联领先的光芒一直是蓝鸟的苦主，本赛季六场赢了五场。坦白说，能打成平手就已经不错了，但蓝鸟之后需要长期赢得系列赛。输掉三场——或者被光芒横扫——可能会迅速改变我们对蓝鸟和交易截止日的讨论。
items.author: Keegan Matheson

items.titleEn: Ward, legendary Blue Jays reliever and two-time champion, dies at 62
bodyEn: TORONTO -- Duane Ward, one of the greatest relievers in Blue Jays history and a two-time World Series champion, has died. He was 62.

Ward was in Toronto this week for the Blue Jays’ celebrations to honor those 1992-93 teams, which included Saturday’s unveiling of the Back-to-Back World Series Statue. Ward was noticeably absent from the ceremonies themselves, though, and the Blue Jays announced early Sunday that he passed away in Toronto from natural causes.

So many Blue Jays alumni spoke of Ward on Saturday, all of them still so grateful for the one-two punch that Ward and Tom Henke formed at the back of the bullpen in 1992, before Henke moved on the following season and Ward took over as the full-time closer. Cito Gaston, the legendary manager, has always joked about how easy his job became when those late innings rolled around.
“The 1992 team, we had just about everything going for us. We only played seven-inning ballgames,” Gaston said, “because we had Ward come in, then David Wells or Tom Henke.”

These past few days had been a portal back to the good old days for so many of these players, alumni and executives. From role players to superstars and even the great GM, Hall of Famer Pat Gillick, they all spoke about how much it meant to be back together one more time. Baseball was rarely the topic of conversation, they all said, just a bunch of old friends catching up.
Ward was a beloved teammate in his playing days and beyond. Over the years, he’d expressed that same feeling, and had been photographed with teammates in Toronto in the days leading up to his death.
“It was one family,” Ward said in a 2020 interview with MLB.com. “We would fight for one another and we would fight against each other. When it came game time, it was all for one and one for all.”

What a pitcher. The Blue Jays acquired Ward in a 1986 trade for Doyle Alexander, and once he established himself in the big leagues in ‘88, he and Henke formed a duo that would fit so well in the modern game. They were ahead of their time, two hard-throwing relievers who could take the ball every single day. It was like having two copies of Louis Varland to cover the seventh, eighth and ninth each night.

“Me and Tom got along great. We didn’t have any animosity towards each other, none of that at all,” Ward said in 2020. “I always joked that I couldn’t stand him, he couldn’t stand me, but we were -- it’s a corny phrase -- but two peas in a pod.”

When Henke departed the Blue Jays following the 1992 World Series win, Ward stepped into the closer role as his own after five seasons straight of double-digit saves and 100-plus games behind Henke. In ‘93, he gave the Blue Jays one of the finest relief seasons we’ve ever seen in this city, posting a 2.13 ERA with 97 strikeouts over 71 2/3 innings.
Ward’s 45 saves that season still stand as the single-season franchise record. For over three decades now, Blue Jays closers have been chasing Ward, but no other has even touched 40.

Ward’s legacy feels so permanent in this city, one of the giants of this organization. Just as hitters all chase down the records of Carlos Delgado or starters chase down Dave Stieb and Roy Halladay, everyone who runs out of the Blue Jays’ bullpen is chasing down Henke and Ward.
items.titleZh: 蓝鸟传奇后援投手、两冠王Ward逝世，享年62岁
items.summaryZh: 多伦多蓝鸟历史上最伟大的后援投手之一、两届世界大赛冠军Duane Ward去世，享年62岁。
bodyZh: 多伦多 -- 蓝鸟队史最伟大的后援投手之一、两届世界大赛冠军Duane Ward去世，享年62岁。
Ward本周在多伦多参加蓝鸟队纪念1992-93年球队的庆祝活动，包括周六揭幕的“背靠背世界大赛”雕像。然而，Ward明显缺席了仪式本身，蓝鸟队周日清晨宣布他在多伦多因自然原因去世。
周六，许多蓝鸟队老将谈及Ward，他们至今仍感激1992年Ward和Tom Henke在牛棚后端形成的组合拳，随后Henke在下一个赛季离队，Ward接任全职终结者。传奇总教练Cito Gaston一直开玩笑说，当比赛进入后段局数时，他的工作变得多么轻松。
“1992年的球队，我们几乎拥有一切。我们只打七局比赛，”Gaston说，“因为Ward会上场，然后David Wells或Tom Henke。”
过去几天对于许多球员、老将和行政人员来说，是一扇回到美好旧时光的大门。从角色球员到超级巨星，甚至伟大的总经理、名人堂成员Pat Gillick，他们都谈到再次聚在一起是多么有意义。他们都说，棒球很少成为话题，只是一群老朋友叙旧。
Ward在球员时代及以后都是一位深受爱戴的队友。多年来，他表达了同样的感受，并在去世前几天在多伦多与队友合影。
“这是一个大家庭，”Ward在2020年接受MLB.com采访时说。“我们会为彼此而战，也会互相竞争。到了比赛时间，就是人人为我，我为人人。”
多么出色的投手。蓝鸟队在1986年用Doyle Alexander交易换来Ward，他在88年站稳大联盟后，与Henke组成了一个非常适合现代比赛的二人组。他们是领先时代的，两位每天都能投球的强力后援投手。就像每晚有两个Louis Varland来覆盖第七、第八和第九局。
“我和Tom相处得很好。我们之间没有任何敌意，完全没有，”Ward在2020年说。“我总开玩笑说我受不了他，他也受不了我，但我们是——这是个老套的说法——但我们是形影不离。”
在1992年世界大赛夺冠后Henke离开蓝鸟队，Ward在连续五个赛季担任Henke身后、交出两位数救援成功和出场100次以上的表现后，接管了终结者角色。1993年，他为蓝鸟队贡献了这座城市所见过的后援投手最佳赛季之一，在71 2/3局中投出2.13自责分率，97次三振。
Ward当季的45次救援成功仍是队史单赛季纪录。三十多年来，蓝鸟队的终结者们一直在追赶Ward，但没有一人能达到40次。
Ward的遗产在这个城市如此永恒，他是这支球队的巨人之一。正如打者追逐Carlos Delgado的纪录、先发投手追逐Dave Stieb和Roy Halladay一样，每一个从蓝鸟牛棚跑出的投手都在追逐Henke和Ward。
items.author: Keegan Matheson

items.titleEn: 'There's a brotherhood': Beloved 1992-93 Blue Jays reunite to celebrate back-to-back titles
bodyEn: TORONTO -- Walking back onto this field, one after the other, the old World Series champions were all 25 years old again.

Saturday wasn’t the first celebration of the 1992-93 teams, who went back-to-back over three decades ago. It won’t be the last, either. Most of them would do this every weekend, if they could. All in town to celebrate the unveiling of the Back-to-Back World Series Statue, they could step back into the best days of their lives.
Joe Carter, leading the group out onto the field after the statue unveiling, couldn’t stop smiling. Has he stopped in the last 32 years?

The moment they stepped back onto the turf, they were ball players again. They looked around in awe, some of them even winding up their arms or joking with one another that it’s time to start stretching, the group of old friends all exchanging a look that said, "I bet I could still do this," even if they know they can’t.

“We sit around and tell a lot of lies,” Carter said, lighting up again. “But you know what? We never talk about baseball now. As we get older, it’s like, ‘How many surgeries have you had? How’s your back? How’s your knee?’ It’s great to see guys like Pat Gillick and Paul Beeston, the nucleus of the team. This is a lot of fun. As we reach that golden age, getting up there to be senior citizens, we relish these moments.”

They’ve all lived other lives since then. Some are grandparents now. Some, like the great Tony Fernandez, have left us too soon. Some have gone into business, others into retirement. But then, they step back on that field.
“It’s like I played with them yesterday,” said Pat Borders, MVP of the 1992 World Series.

Yesterday on one of their buses, Dave Stewart stood up and spoke with the group, reminding them just how important their lives are to one another’s. Those World Series wins last forever, and so do these relationships.

“There’s a brotherhood,” Borders said. “Even if we don’t see each other for a couple of years, or maybe even more than a couple of years, we come back and it’s like you just saw them yesterday. These are true brothers and you have a great love for them.”

From 1992 to ‘93, the Blue Jays completely overhauled their roster, led by general manager Pat Gillick. Longtime big-league star Paul Molitor was brought in for the second run in ‘93, and players with rings already on their fingers rallied around getting him one of his own.

A baseball lifer in every sense, the old player still comes out in Molitor when he remembers that famous Game 6 in 1993. He was standing on first base in the bottom of the ninth, the best view in the house, and he still remembers every detail.

“I got a hit off Mitch Williams and Rickey [Henderson] had walked,” Molitor remembers. “My thought was that, if something happens here, I’m going to be the winning run in the World Series. A double in the gap, maybe a roller down the line. When Joe hit it, it wasn’t a high, majestic homer. It was a line drive. I was thinking that if that ball hits the wall, I’ve got to score…”

None of them will get tired of these stories, and how could they? Carter has told these same stories 100,000 times, and he hopes he’ll be able to tell them 100,000 more. That statue standing outside now, capturing the most famous moment in Blue Jays history, will live there forever.

These fierce competitors soften with time, too. Even the great Dave Stieb, so famous for his piercing glare and a level of competitiveness that often became a monster of its own, found a moment to appreciate this scene between the jokes and jabs.
“This is great. It’s always great to see everybody,” Stieb said. “We share some stories, you see how their personal lives are going now.”

For so long, these teams have represented what’s possible in this city. They’ve had to carry that torch for too long now, and were nearly joined by the 2025 team, but they’re happy to keep carrying it. For them, the World Series years aren’t just videos, famous radio calls and blurry memories. They’re a real thing, and on days like this, they can all step back into the past together.
“We are back in those days,” Borders said.
items.titleZh: “兄弟情谊”：备受喜爱的1992-93蓝鸟队重聚庆祝连冠
items.summaryZh: 1992-93蓝鸟冠军队员重聚揭幕连冠雕像，回忆光辉岁月，兄弟情谊深厚。
bodyZh: 多伦多——当这些往届世界大赛冠军一个接一个重新踏上这片球场时，他们仿佛又回到了25岁。
周六并不是1992-93赛季球队的首次庆祝，三十多年前他们曾连续夺冠。这也不会是最后一次。如果可以，他们中的大多数人愿意每个周末都这样做。所有人齐聚多伦多，庆祝“背靠背世界大赛雕像”揭幕，得以重温人生中最美好的时光。
乔·卡特在雕像揭幕后带领队伍出场，笑容满面。过去32年里他笑过吗？
当他们再次踏上草皮的那一刻，他们又成了球员。他们敬畏地环顾四周，有些人甚至活动着手臂，互相开玩笑说该开始拉伸了。这群老友交换着眼神，仿佛在说：“我打赌我还能做到。”尽管他们知道已经不行了。
“我们坐在一起讲很多大话，”卡特笑着说，“但你知道吗？现在我们从不谈棒球。随着年龄增长，话题变成了‘你做过几次手术？后背怎么样？膝盖怎么样？’见到像帕特·吉列克和保罗·比斯顿这样的球队核心成员真是太好了。这非常有趣。当我们步入黄金年代，逐渐成为老年人时，我们珍惜这些时刻。”
自那以后，他们都过上了不同的生活。有些人现在已是祖父母。有些人，如伟大的托尼·费尔南德斯，已过早离世。有些人从商，有些人退休。但一旦踏上这片球场，一切又回到从前。
“就好像昨天还和他们一起打球，”1992年世界大赛MVP帕特·博德斯说。
昨天在一辆大巴上，戴夫·斯图尔特站起来对大家说，提醒他们彼此的人生多么重要。那些世界大赛的胜利永恒不灭，这些关系也同样持久。
“这是一种兄弟情谊，”博德斯说，“即使我们几年不见，甚至更久，再聚首时就像昨天刚见过。他们是真正的兄弟，你对他们怀有深深的爱。”
从1992年到1993年，蓝鸟队在总经理帕特·吉列克的带领下彻底调整了阵容。1993年第二次夺冠时，他们引进了长期大联盟球星保罗·莫里托尔，已经拥有戒指的球员们齐心协力为他赢得一枚。
一个不折不扣的棒球人，当莫里托尔回忆起1993年那场著名的第六战时，老球员的本色依旧流露。他当时站在一垒，在第九局下半，那是全场最佳视角，他至今记得每个细节。
“我从米奇·威廉姆斯手中击出安打，里基（亨德森）保送上垒，”莫里托尔回忆道，“我当时想，如果接下来发生点什么，我可能成为世界大赛的制胜分。可能是一支穿越空旷场地的二垒安打，也可能是一支沿着边线滚动的安打。当乔击出球时，那并不是一记高飞的 majestic 本垒打，而是一记平飞球。我在想，如果球撞到墙上，我必须得分……”
没有人会听腻这些故事，他们怎么会呢？卡特已经讲过这些故事十万次了，他希望还能再讲十万次。如今矗立在场外的雕像，捕捉了蓝鸟队史上最著名的时刻，将永远屹立在那里。
时间也让这些激烈的竞争者变得温柔。即使是著名的戴夫·斯蒂布，以锐利的目光和常常如猛兽般的竞争心闻名，也在玩笑和调侃之间找到片刻来欣赏这一幕。
“这太棒了。能见到大家总是很棒，”斯蒂布说，“我们分享故事，看看他们现在的生活如何。”
长期以来，这些球队代表着这座城市的一切可能。他们背负这火炬已经太久，2025年的球队差点加入其中，但他们很高兴继续扛起它。对他们而言，世界大赛的年份不仅仅是视频、著名的电台解说和模糊的记忆。它们是真实的，在这样的日子里，他们可以一起回到过去。
“我们回到了那些日子，”博德斯说。
items.author: Keegan Matheson

items.titleEn: Vlad shakes off dust, Bieber spins six scoreless in Blue Jays win
bodyEn: TORONTO -- When your franchise unveils its first player statue outside the stadium and celebrates its two World Series teams in a sprawling pregame ceremony, you’d better find a way to win the game that follows.
The Blue Jays did just that, beating the White Sox, 1-0, in front of the franchise’s most beloved alumni.
This is what mattered most from the game that followed the celebrations:
1. Signs of life from Vladimir Guerrero Jr.

When he made contact, it looked like Vladdy had finally hit his first home run at Rogers Centre in 2026. The moment would have been so perfect, too, just hours after embracing Joe Carter on the field after the Blue Jays unveiled their back-to-back World Series Statue.

He had to settle for a 402-foot double, then came around to score soon after, when George Springer rolled an RBI single back up the middle. Add in another single and a walk? This was a fine day at the ballpark for Guerrero.

This also comes after Guerrero made some very loud outs in the opener against the White Sox on Friday, so there are finally some arrows pointed in a positive direction here. It’s no secret that the Blue Jays need Guerrero to be the man leading this turnaround, and unless he rediscovers some shred of his 2025 form, that hill may be too steep to climb. The home runs need to come next -- and they need to come quickly -- but this was one of Guerrero’s better games in quite some time.
2. Shane Bieber, at last
This is what the Blue Jays have been waiting for.

Bieber gave the Blue Jays six shutout innings with six strikeouts and just three hits allowed, his best start since returning from the IL last month and a massive step in the right direction. Bieber owned a 7.64 ERA over his first four starts and had run into home run troubles, but this time, he finally looked like the control artist the Blue Jays acquired a year ago from the Guardians at the Trade Deadline.

At this point, the Blue Jays can’t afford to have one more starter stumble, and there have been some worrying signs for many of them. Dylan Cease has been brilliant, but Bieber’s return had been rocky. Kevin Gausman has put up some uncharacteristically poor starts recently and Trey Yesavage walked seven batters his last time out. The No. 5 job belongs to Spencer Miles, the Rule 5 wonder who is essentially a swingman, so the Blue Jays need the other four established starters to be rock solid.
Bieber, at last, gave the Blue Jays just that.
3. Play of the game: Kirk with a dart

In a one-run game, you need to play airtight baseball, something the Blue Jays have struggled with terribly in 2026. Saturday’s win looked a little more like the ‘25 style, making all of the right plays at the right time.

There was none better than Alejandro Kirk in the top of the eighth, cutting down an attempted steal after the leadoff hitter reached on a bunt. Kirk placed this throw perfectly, set up well for his throw by an outside pitch from Tyler Rogers, and this has to be an extremely encouraging sign for the Blue Jays.

Teams have been running all over Kirk this season, who had thrown out just six of 40 attempted steals (15%) coming into play. Part of this is a carryover from a year ago, while part of it can be tied to his fractured thumb, which cost him significant time earlier in the season and can be difficult to return to 100% from. Either way, the Blue Jays need to control the running game better than they do, and Kirk needs to be at the center of that.
items.titleZh: 弗拉基米尔挥别尘埃，比伯六局无失分助蓝鸟取胜
items.summaryZh: 小葛瑞菲状态回暖，比伯六局无失分，蓝鸟1-0险胜白袜
bodyZh: 多伦多——当球队在球场外揭晓首座球员雕像，并在盛大的赛前仪式上庆祝两届世界大赛冠军时，你必须想办法赢下随后的比赛。
蓝鸟做到了，在球队最受爱戴的校友面前以1-0击败白袜。
以下是庆祝活动后这场比赛最重要的内容：
1. Vladimir Guerrero Jr. 出现复苏迹象
当他击到球时，看起来Vladdy终于要在2026年于Rogers Centre击出首支本垒打了。这个时刻本应是如此完美，就在蓝鸟揭晓背靠背世界大赛雕像数小时后，他在球场上拥抱了Joe Carter。
他只能满足于一支402英尺的二垒安打，随后不久便跑回得分，George Springer打出打点一垒安打穿过中路。再加上一支一垒安打和一次保送？这对Guerrero来说是球场上的美好一天。
此前，Guerrero在周五对阵白袜的揭幕战中打出了一些非常响亮的出局，所以现在终于有一些积极的迹象。蓝鸟需要Guerrero成为引领这次反弹的人，这已不是秘密，除非他能找回2025年状态的一丝痕迹，否则爬坡可能太陡。接下来必须打出本垒打——而且需要尽快——但这是Guerrero相当长一段时间以来表现较好的比赛之一。
2. Shane Bieber，终于来了
这正是蓝鸟一直等待的。
Bieber为蓝鸟投出六局无失分，六次三振，仅被击出三支安打，这是他自上个月从伤病名单回归以来最好的先发，也是朝着正确方向迈出的一大步。Bieber在前四次先发中自责分率为7.64，并且一直遇到本垒打麻烦，但这一次，他终于看起来像是蓝鸟一年前在交易截止日从守护者交易来的控球大师。
此时此刻，蓝鸟再也经不起另一位先发投手的挣扎，而其中许多人已经出现了一些令人担忧的迹象。Dylan Cease一直很出色，但Bieber的回归一直不稳定。Kevin Gausman最近几次先发表现失常，Trey Yesavage上次先发保送了七名打者。第五号先发属于Spencer Miles，这位规则5选秀的惊喜人物基本上是摇摆人，所以蓝鸟需要其他四位既定先发投手坚如磐石。
Bieber，终于，给了蓝鸟这样的表现。
3. 比赛关键时刻：Kirk的精准传球
在一分差的比赛中，你必须打出滴水不漏的棒球，这是蓝鸟在2026年一直非常挣扎的。周六的胜利看起来更像是25年的风格，在正确的时间做出正确的表现。
没有比Alejandro Kirk在第八局上半更好的表现了，在首名打者靠触击上垒后，他阻杀了一次尝试盗垒。Kirk的传球完美到位，凭借Tyler Rogers的偏外角球路为他的传球做好了准备，这对蓝鸟来说必须是一个极其令人鼓舞的迹象。
本赛季各队一直在Kirk面前盗垒，他赛前在40次尝试盗垒中只阻杀了6次（15%）。部分原因是去年的延续，部分原因可以归咎于他的拇指骨折，这让他赛季初缺席了很长时间，并且很难100%恢复。无论如何，蓝鸟需要比现在更好地控制跑垒，而Kirk必须成为其中的核心。
items.author: Keegan Matheson

items.titleEn: Blue Jays honor 1992-93 back-to-back championship teams with new statue outside Rogers Centre
bodyEn: TORONTO -- When you win in this city, it lasts forever. Finally, outside the gates of Rogers Centre, stands a statue that will do the same.

The Blue Jays unveiled their “Back-to-Back World Series Statue” Saturday afternoon, the first statue of a player to stand outside of this stadium in the club’s 50-year history. Who better than Joe Carter? What better than one of the greatest moments in baseball history?

Carter’s walk-off home run to win the 1993 World Series will forever be this franchise’s iconic moment. Perhaps someday it will be rivaled, but never topped. Now, it will stand forever between Gate 5 and 6, a monument to Carter and his Blue Jays teammates who went back-to-back. Each day now, fans will crowd around for photos, some of them who remember exactly where they were when Carter made contact, and so many of whom weren’t even born in 1993.

“We were all part of something truly special, and I’m forever grateful to share this moment with our fans and my incredible teammates from 1992 and 1993,” said Carter. “When I reflect on the impact our back-to-back championships had on fans – not just in Toronto, but across Canada – it brings tears to my eyes. To be honoured in this way alongside my teammates and to be part of history is deeply humbling.”

Unveiled outside just prior to first pitch, Sportsnet’s Hazel Mae led the ceremony before turning the mic over to Carter and the great Cito Gaston, who managed those World Series teams. The statue, which stands 15 feet tall and weighs 6,200 pounds, depicts Carter leaping into the air in celebration, one of those unforgettable snapshots in Blue Jays history.

Along the statue’s granite base, the 1992 and ‘93 rosters are listed, showcasing not only the incredible talent the Blue Jays boasted in those years, but also the amount of turnover between World Series years, a masterclass by Pat Gillick and the front office.
On another side, it’s those famous words. Over the years, Carter has broken into that bright smile so many times and recited the late, great Tom Cheek, radio voice of the Blue Jays from 1977-2004.
“Touch 'em all, Joe! You’ll never hit a bigger home run in your life!”

This statue continues a run of excellent work done by the organization to both preserve and promote this team’s history in its 50th season, something fans have long been asking for. Displays now line the 100 Level concourse, honoring different eras of this club’s history, and on Aug. 29, the Blue Jays will unveil their Hall of Excellence, honoring Buck Martinez and members of their Level of Excellence.

“Defending a championship is one of the rarest accomplishments in sports – a moment that transcends the game and becomes part of a community’s shared history,” said club president and CEO Mark Shapiro. “It deserves to be celebrated, cherished, and passed down through generations of fans. ... As we celebrate our 50th season, this statue stands as a tribute to a defining moment in our franchise and to the fans who made it meaningful and have carried that memory with them for decades. This statue is, and always will be, for the fans.”

After the ceremony outdoors, Carter and Gaston led alumni from the ‘92 and ‘93 teams back into the stadium, down through the bleachers and out onto the field, where Carter addressed the crowd once again and fired the ceremonial first pitch to George Springer. Vladimir Guerrero Jr. then came out to join the celebration, gifting Carter his own version of their “home run jacket,” this one bedazzled with “Touch ‘em all, Joe!” across the back.

The ceremony was attended by 28 players, coaches, and front-office personnel from the 1992 and 1993 teams: Al Leiter, Alfredo Griffin, Candy Maldonado, Danny Cox, Dave Stewart, Dave Stieb, Devon White, Ed Sprague, Gene Tenace, Jack Morris, Joe Carter, John Olerud, Juan Guzman, Kelly Gruber, Nick Leyva, Pat Borders, Pat Hentgen, Pat Tabler, Paul Molitor, Rance Mulliniks, Rob Butler, Todd Stottlemyre, Tom Henke, Turner Ward, manager Cito Gaston, assistant general manager Gord Ash, general manager Pat Gillick, and president Paul Beeston.
items.titleZh: 蓝鸟在罗杰斯中心外树立新雕像致敬1992-93连冠球队
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟在罗杰斯中心外揭幕“背靠背世界大赛雕像”，纪念乔·卡特1993年再见本垒打。
bodyZh: 多伦多——在这座城市，胜利是永恒的。如今，罗杰斯中心大门外终于矗立起一座同样永恒的雕像。
周六下午，蓝鸟队揭幕了“背靠背世界大赛雕像”，这是球队50年历史中首座矗立于球场外的球员雕像。还有谁比乔·卡特更合适？还有什么比棒球史上最伟大的时刻之一更恰当？
卡特在1993年世界大赛中击出的再见本垒打将永远是这支球队的标志性时刻。也许有一天会有对手，但永远不会被超越。如今，它将永远矗立在5号和6号门之间，成为卡特及其蓝鸟队友们连续夺冠的纪念碑。每天，球迷们都会围上来拍照，其中有些人清楚记得卡特击球时自己在哪里，而更多的人在1993年尚未出生。
“我们都是真正特别事物的一部分，我永远感激能与我们的球迷以及1992年和1993年那些不可思议的队友分享这一刻，”卡特说。“当我回想起我们的连续夺冠对球迷——不仅是在多伦多，而是整个加拿大——产生的影响时，我热泪盈眶。能以这种方式与队友一同受到表彰，成为历史的一部分，让我深感谦卑。”
在第一球开打前，雕像在球场外揭幕，Sportsnet的Hazel Mae主持了仪式，随后将话筒交给卡特和执教这两支世界大赛球队的伟大教练Cito Gaston。这座雕像高15英尺，重6200磅，描绘了卡特跃起庆祝的瞬间，那是蓝鸟队史中令人难忘的画面之一。
在雕像的花岗岩基座上，列出了1992年和1993年的球员名单，不仅展示了蓝鸟当年拥有的惊人天赋，也体现了两个世界大赛年份之间的人员变动，堪称Pat Gillick和管理层的杰作。
另一面则刻着那句著名的话语。多年来，卡特无数次绽放灿烂笑容，复述着已故的伟大解说员Tom Cheek（1977-2004年蓝鸟电台解说）的名言。
“全垒打，乔！你这辈子再也不会打出更大的本垒打了！”
这座雕像是球队在其第50个赛季为保护和弘扬球队历史所做的一系列出色工作的一部分，这也是球迷们长久以来的期盼。如今，100层看台走廊上陈列着展示不同时代球队历史的展品，8月29日，蓝鸟将揭幕卓越荣誉厅，表彰Buck Martinez以及卓越荣誉榜成员。
“卫冕冠军是体育界最罕见的成就之一——它超越了比赛本身，成为社区共同历史的一部分，”俱乐部主席兼首席执行官Mark Shapiro说。“它值得被庆祝、珍视，并代代相传。……在我们庆祝第50个赛季之际，这座雕像既是对球队决定性时刻的致敬，也是对球迷们的感谢，是他们让这一刻意义非凡，并将这份记忆珍藏了数十年。这座雕像现在，也将永远，属于球迷。”
在室外仪式结束后，卡特和Gaston带领1992年和1993年的校友回到球场内，穿过看台来到场地上，卡特再次向人群致辞，并投出 ceremonial first pitch 给 George Springer。Vladimir Guerrero Jr. 随后加入庆祝，送给卡特他自己版本的“本垒打夹克”，背面镶有“全垒打，乔！”字样。

出席仪式的有28名来自1992年和1993年球队的球员、教练和管理人员：Al Leiter、Alfredo Griffin、Candy Maldonado、Danny Cox、Dave Stewart、Dave Stieb、Devon White、Ed Sprague、Gene Tenace、Jack Morris、Joe Carter、John Olerud、Juan Guzman、Kelly Gruber、Nick Leyva、Pat Borders、Pat Hentgen、Pat Tabler、Paul Molitor、Rance Mulliniks、Rob Butler、Todd Stottlemyre、Tom Henke、Turner Ward、主教练Cito Gaston、助理总经理Gord Ash、总经理Pat Gillick和总裁Paul Beeston。
items.author: Keegan Matheson

items.titleEn: As Trade Deadline looms, where could Miles end up on Toronto's staff?
bodyEn: TORONTO -- With the clock ticking on the Blue Jays and the Trade Deadline looming on Aug. 3, Friday night wasn’t the grand return from the break they’d envisioned.
From their 12-4 loss to the White Sox, dropping them to 45-52, this is what mattered most:
1. Is Spencer Miles a starter now?

It already feels like this answer will be a firm “yes” by the spring of 2027, but coming out of the break, Miles is toeing the line between being a swingman, a bulk guy and a starter. This game loves labels, doesn’t it?

Friday’s line was a rare bump in the road for Miles, who has otherwise been one of this organization’s best stories. The Rule 5 Draft wonder allowed six runs over his four-plus innings, five of which came in the second. That inning was a mixed bag, though. Sam Antonacci’s home run can’t be excused away, but before that ball left the yard, the White Sox built their inning by finding some holes with ground balls and a bloop double.
“Stopping the bleeding, you need to start missing bats,” Miles said. “Getting more swing and miss, maybe a different pitch. A lot of it’s bad luck, and then I gave up the two-run homer.”

Miles cranked it back up to 60 pitches on Friday, his most since June 6, and he should be able to push that closer to his career high (73) if he makes another traditional start the next time through. For now, you can expect that to happen unless the Blue Jays are desperate for a multi-inning reliever over the next four days.
“I felt really good. I was kind of hoping they’d let me go longer,” Miles said, “but just for safety wise, that’s probably all I had in me.”

Whether the Blue Jays buy or sell at the Trade Deadline will impact Miles heavily. If they buy, likely adding a starter, he’ll slide back to the bullpen in a valuable role. If they sell, which could include a starter on the way out, he could earn that permanent label a little earlier.
2. A quiet return for the bats
This team can only make a run if the lineup turns this around, and Friday night wasn’t a strong start.

Even as White Sox starter Anthony Kay held the door wide open for them with walks and hit batters, the Blue Jays couldn’t find that one big hit, which is too common a story this season. They still rank tied for 25th in runs scored (396) and 22nd in home runs (101).

“We’ve just got to get the big hit,” manager John Schneider said. “Offensively, you [have to] continue to trend in the right direction. You’ve got to get the big hit or hit homers with guys on. That’s been the ‘thing’ all year.”

The most encouraging swing? George Springer turned on a fastball in the fifth inning and launched his 10th of the season to left field. This team desperately needs Vladimir Guerrero Jr., but beyond Vladdy, Springer is another who can singlehandedly change the energy in this lineup.
3. Look at who’s in left field…
For the first time this season, Springer needed to take his glove to work.

Making his first start in the outfield since Sept. 24, 2025, Springer seemed energized by the return pregame, going back-and-forth with Blue Jays fans and his own bullpen in left field prior to first pitch. The 36-year-old isn’t as fast as he was at 24, but still has excellent ball skills and 13 years of big-league experience to lean on, which is more than enough.

“We’re trying to do it with lefties on the mound to get [Brandon] Valenzuela [at DH] and Kirk in there,” Schneider said. “We’ve talked about it for a few weeks. He’s had some spurts with the toe [injury] and being away with his baby, so we wanted to make sure he was good enough to do it. This won’t be every day. He’s at the point where he’s comfortable at DH and he’s not that young, so we don’t want to run him out there and kill him. But to use our whole roster a bit differently, we can put him out there a couple of times every couple of weeks and rotate the DH spot.”
For Day 1, the idea worked. Soon after Springer homered in the fifth, Valenzuela launched his first home run since June 7 in the next inning.
items.titleZh: 交易截止日临近，迈尔斯在蓝鸟的角色将何去何从？
items.summaryZh: 斯宾塞·迈尔斯周五投4局失6分，蓝鸟12-4不敌白袜。
bodyZh: 多伦多——随着蓝鸟的时间紧迫，8月3日的交易截止日日益临近，周五晚绝非他们预想中的全明星假期后的盛大回归。
从他们12-4输给白袜、战绩跌至45胜52负的比赛来看，以下几点最为重要：
1. 斯宾塞·迈尔斯现在算是先发投手吗？
感觉这个答案到2027年春天就会是坚定的“是”，但全明星假期刚过，迈尔斯正处在摇摆人、长局投手和先发投手之间的模糊地带。这项运动很喜欢贴标签，不是吗？
周五的比赛是迈尔斯罕见的一次磕绊，而他此前一直是球队最亮眼的故事之一。这位规则5选秀的惊喜之作在4局多的投球中失6分，其中5分来自第二局。不过那一局也是好坏参半。萨姆·安托纳奇的阳春炮无可推诿，但在此之前，白袜依靠滚地球找到空档和一记高飞二垒安打构筑了攻势。
“要止血，你必须开始让打者挥空，”迈尔斯说，“更多的挥空，也许换个球种。其中很多是运气不好，然后我被打了两分炮。”
迈尔斯周五的用球数达到了60球，是自6月6日以来的最高，如果下一次他再以传统先发身份出场，应该能将球数推近自己的生涯最高（73球）。就目前看来，除非蓝鸟在接下来四天急需一位多局后援，你预计他会以先发身份登场。
“我感觉非常好。我本来希望他们让我投更久，”迈尔斯说，“但出于安全考虑，那可能就是我当时的极限了。”
蓝鸟在交易截止日是买是卖将严重影响迈尔斯的处境。如果他们交易补强，很可能会增加一位先发投手，那么他就会回到牛棚担任有价值的角色。如果他们交易卖人，可能包括送走一位先发投手，那么他可能更早获得那个永久标签。
2. 打线回归冷淡
这支球队要想有所作为，必须靠打线扭转局面，而周五晚的开局并不强劲。
即使白袜先发安东尼·凯用保送和触身球为他们敞开大门，蓝鸟仍无法敲出关键一击，这是本赛季常见的故事。他们的得分（396分）并列排名第25，本垒打（101支）排名第22。
“我们就是需要关键安打，”主教练约翰·施奈德说，“进攻端，你必须继续朝正确的方向前进。你需要在垒上有人时打出关键安打或本垒打。这是整个赛季以来的‘问题’。”
最鼓舞人心的挥棒？乔治·斯普林格在第五局抓住一颗速球，将本季第10轰送到左外野。这支球队急需小弗拉基米尔·格雷罗，但除了Vladdy，斯普林格是另一个能单枪匹马改变打线能量的人。
3. 看看谁在左外野……
本赛季第一次，斯普林格需要带上手套去工作。
自2025年9月24日以来首次在外野先发，斯普林格在赛前显得精神振奋，与蓝鸟球迷以及自己在左外野的牛棚来回互动。这位36岁的老将不如24岁时快速，但仍有出色的球感和13年的大联盟经验可以依靠，这已经足够了。
“我们尝试在面对左投时让[布兰登]巴伦苏埃拉担任指定打击，同时让柯克上场，”施奈德说，“我们已经讨论了几周。他因脚趾伤病和照顾孩子有过一些间歇，所以我们想确保他的状态足够好。这不会每天都发生。他现在处于对DH感到舒适的阶段，而且他也不再年轻，所以我们不想让他天天上场而累垮他。但为了更灵活地使用我们的整个阵容，我们可以每两周让他上场几次，轮换DH位置。”
第一天，这个想法奏效了。在斯普林格第五局开轰后不久，巴伦苏埃拉在下一局打出了自6月7日以来的第一支本垒打。
items.author: Keegan Matheson

items.titleEn: 1 key player for each team in the second half
bodyEn: The second half of the MLB season usually comes with a mission. For contenders, it's getting to the postseason. For others, it's taking stock and looking ahead to improve for next season.

In either circumstance, there are players who teams want to see contribute because they figure to be an important part of the future, one way or another. With help from MLB.com's beat writers, here's a look at one player from each team who will be key in the second half.
Jump to: AL East | AL Central | AL West | NL East | NL Central | NL West
AL EAST
Blue Jays: 1B Vladimir Guerrero Jr.

This hasn’t changed all season. If Vladdy can get back to even 75% of the hitter he was in the 2025 postseason, an entire world of possibilities opens up for the Blue Jays. If that doesn’t happen, they’d need four or five other hitters to all get hot at the same time. No player in this organization has more power to change the trajectory of the Blue Jays than Vladdy, but this wait has stretched on for nearly 100 games by now. -- Keegan Matheson
Orioles: SS Gunnar Henderson

It’s Henderson, without a doubt. The 25-year-old star shortstop has had a challenging season thus far, hitting .224 with a .697 OPS through 96 games. His power has still been there (17 homers, which ranks second on the team), but his level of play has been down from the first three full seasons of his MLB career. Henderson may be turning things around, though, as he went 5-for-13 (.385) during last weekend’s three-game sweep of the Royals, finishing the series with a three-hit performance on Sunday. If he can keep it going, it would be a huge boost for the O’s moving forward. -- Jake Rill
Rays: LF Chandler Simpson

Simpson is not necessarily the Rays’ most important player. Their lineup is much more dependent upon Yandy Díaz, Jonathan Aranda and Junior Caminero. It’s hard to imagine their rotation without Drew Rasmussen, Shane McClanahan, Nick Martinez or, now, Griffin Jax. And their bullpen would be in trouble without Bryan Baker and Kevin Kelly. But Simpson is a wild card, the difference between their lineup being fine and being dangerous. When he’s hitting for a high average, making the defense panic and wreaking havoc on the bases, the Rays are a different team. -- Adam Berry
Red Sox: OF Roman Anthony and LHP Garrett Crochet

While a lot of the roster has come together during the 16-2 hot streak, the player the Red Sox miss most is Anthony, who hasn’t played since May 4 due to a partially torn tendon in his right hand/wrist. If Anthony can turn a corner soon and be back for the last six weeks or so, it could have a big impact on a Boston team that has relied heavily on pitching for most of the season. And as stellar as that pitching has been, the return of injured ace Crochet – even if not until September – could give Boston a big boost. -- Ian Browne
Yankees: OF Aaron Judge

No addition the Yankees could make between now and the end of the season compares to getting Aaron Judge back healthy. The captain and three-time MVP hasn’t played since late May and the offense has sputtered for extended periods without him. Judge had his fractured right rib re-imaged during the All-Star break but he’ll need more time to heal. The best case scenario appears to be Judge returning sometime in August, but don’t rule out September. -- Bryan Hoch
AL Central
Guardians: 3B José Ramírez

No surprise here. Ramírez has advanced into a hitting progression in his rehab from surgery, and it may not be long before he begins a rehab assignment. For as much as Cleveland has done well treading water without the 33-year-old, it’s anxiously awaiting his return. The Guardians’ lineup is on a higher level with the presence of its superstar third baseman in the middle of the order. -- Tim Stebbins
Royals: 1B Vinnie Pasquantino

Pasquantino’s first half was abysmal, and he’ll be the first one to tell you that. He struggled immensely not just to produce power in the middle of the lineup, but production overall, and then he dealt with a hamate injury before returning right before the break. If he can look more like his 2025 version in the second half of this year, it will give the Royals confidence in his future role. -- Anne Rogers
Tigers: 2B Gleyber Torres

The Tigers’ offense looked a lot more dangerous when Torres returned from a left oblique strain on June 2, only to see him go back on the injured list with an aggravation 15 days later. His plate discipline and contact make him a natural fit at the top of the order and deepens the lineup behind him, and he gives the lineup a much-needed veteran right-handed bat against lefties. He begins a rehab assignment this week and could be back shortly after the break. -- Jason Beck
Twins: C Ryan Jeffers

So much revolves around Jeffers, who is confident he’ll be himself once he gets going again after spending more than a month and a half on the injured list. But you just never know when a hitter is coming back from a wrist injury. If he can regain the All-Star-type form he showed before he got hurt, it will take an already potent offense to new heights. -- Matthew Leach
White Sox: 1B Munetaka Murakami

Simply put, the slugging first baseman from Japan has changed the franchise in so many positive ways. Murakami missed 35 games before the All-Star break due to a Grade 2 right hamstring strain, with the White Sox posting a 17-18 record in his absence. He went 1-for-11 with seven strikeouts in his return this past weekend, but the White Sox also comfortably won all three games over the A’s. His mere presence helps the lineup and gives a greater overall feeling for victory. -- Scott Merkin
AL West
Angels: 3B Denzer Guzman

Just 22 years old, Guzman has been getting an extended look at third base and has shown some flashes of his immense potential. He’s a natural shortstop but has fared well at the hot corner and has held his own at the plate. He’s batted .262/.307/.411 with four homers, four doubles and 14 RBIs in 27 games, and his development is vital to the organization. -- Rhett Bollinger
Astros: OF Brice Matthews

The Astros entered Sunday with baseball’s third-worst OPS from their outfielders. The club is looking for anybody to establish themselves in left field or center field, which is where Matthews has been splitting time. Matthews has emerged as a terrific defensive outfielder with a strong arm, but the offense would look much better if he could take a step forward at the plate, especially at Daikin Park. -- Brian McTaggart
Athletics: 1B Nick Kurtz

Even as he was slumping before landing on the injured list, Kurtz was putting together an MVP-type season. He’s the type of young star who can carry a team on his back, and that’s exactly what the A’s will need him to do to keep their dwindling playoff hopes alive. If Kurtz’s absence prolongs, the A’s may have no choice but to become sellers. -- Martín Gallegos
Mariners: CF Julio Rodríguez

Second-half Julio is one of the very best players in the league. Dating to his 2022 rookie season, he ranks 13th in OPS (.902) and eighth in homers (59) among qualified hitters after the All-Star break. The bigger concern is how he recovers from a concussion that sidelined him the final nine games of the first half, and how much longer he could be out. -- Daniel Kramer
Rangers: SS Corey Seager and LF Wyatt Langford

The Rangers’ two best hitters in Seager (back, concussion) and Langford (forearm, hamstring) have only played a total of 25 games together this season. That’s not exactly a recipe for success. The Rangers have treaded water well without them and have kept themselves in the thick of the AL West race, but if they want to make a true push, they need the superstars to be superstars.

“The guys that you dreamed of in Spring Training, like what it could look like altogether, we just haven't had it,” manager Skip Schumaker said. “Corey and Wyatt, together, I don't know what the percentage of games they’ve played together, but it can't be that many. That's been challenging for sure. It’s a lot of credit to the other guys that have filled in. We've been doing a really good job of just surviving -- more than survive, quite honestly -- until they get back. I think we've done an exceptional job of that.” -- Kennedi Landry
NL East
Braves: 3B Austin Riley

Riley looked like a perennial MVP candidate when he produced his third straight 30-homer season in 2023. But he has produced a .719 OPS while totaling 44 homers over the three years that have followed. Injuries (broken hand in 2024 and abdominal strain in 2025) have hindered him. But he’s healthy this year and still entered the break with a .618 OPS. A second-half turnaround could significantly improve Atlanta’s lineup. -- Mark Bowman
Marlins: RHP Anthony Bender and LHP John King

The Marlins will ride the Big 3 of Sandy Alcantara, All-Star Max Meyer and Eury Pérez in the rotation and middle-infield duo Otto Lopez and second baseman Xavier Edwards. Arguably most important to the club's success, however, will be the return of injured relievers Bender and King to help a strong but taxed bullpen. Bender was the club's primary setup man, while King proved to be one of the NL's most reliable lefties. -- Christina De Nicola
Mets: RHP Nolan McLean

Even in a disappointing season, McLean has flashed the type of frontline potential that made him one of the NL Rookie of the Year favorites heading into the year. Though McLean’s command of his arsenal has been suspect, he entered the break with better control and a 2.35 ERA over his last eight starts. Continued improvement from him would set the Mets on a better course heading into 2027. -- Anthony DiComo
Nationals: OF James Wood

Wood, 23, is the engine that powers the Nationals’ offense. He already set a franchise record for the most lead-off home runs in a single season (10) — before the All-Star Break. The 6-foot-6, two-time All-Star leads the league in runs scored (89) and walks (79), and he ranks first in the NL in on-base percentage (.410), slugging percentage (.575) and OPS (.985). Wood has the ability to change the course of a Nationals game with one swing of the bat. -- Jessica Camerato
Phillies: RHP Brad Keller, LHP José Alvarado or RHP Orion Kerkering?

It’s Keller, or Alvarado, or even Kerkering. The Phillies need to see more from their late-inning relievers, if they want to avoid more crushing late-inning losses in October. Manager Don Mattingly said he feels good about his “plus” guys in the bullpen, especially if the rotation pitches well in the second half. -- Todd Zolecki
NL Central
Brewers: RHP Jacob Misiorowski

The Brewers have never had a pitcher like him. Perhaps nobody has ever had a pitcher like him. And the whole case for Milwaukee as a World Series contender revolves around delivering the 24-year-old MLB leader in ERA, strikeouts, WHIP and opponents’ average to the postseason at full strength. That’s going to require some creativity in the coming months, beginning with what’s expected to be an extended breather over the All-Star break and into the second half. It’s going to be critical that the Brewers have enough pitching around Misiorowski to manage his workload leading to October. -- Adam McCalvy
Cardinals: SS Masyn Winn

His defense remains Platinum Glove quality, but he has regressed offensively in each of his three full seasons in the majors. He showed improvement at the plate in June and the first part of July; if he can just get back up to a league-average hitter, he’ll be an All-Star quality player who could be the next player the team builds around. -- Will Leitch
Cubs: RHP Daniel Palencia

The Cubs entered the year with high hopes for closer Daniel Palencia, but injuries have limited the hard-throwing righty to 19 games and 16 2/3 innings so far. The bullpen has lacked a consistent end point as a result, as evidenced by 10 pitchers notching at least one save so far. Palencia was a force as the Cubs’ closer in ‘25 and then an impact, multi-inning fireman in the playoffs. He starred for Venezuela in the World Baseball Classic, too. Getting him healthy for the stretch run could do wonders for the ‘pen. -- Jordan Bastian
Pirates: OF Oneil Cruz

Before missing more than a month with fractures in his left hand, Cruz was on pace for a historic 40-homer, 60-stolen base season. Only Ronald Acuña Jr. and Shohei Ohtani have completed the feat. In his best season yet, Cruz is expected to return from the injured list shortly after the break. With the Pirates already having a top offense in baseball, Cruz would only add fuel to the fire. -- Aiden Stepansky
Reds: OF Héctor Rodríguez

Cincinnati's No. 5 prospect, Rodríguez could be the next to make his big league debut at some point in the second half. The lefty slugger, who was acquired from the Mets organization for Tyler Naquin before the 2022 Trade Deadline, has 23 home runs in 89 games, including one three-homer game and three two-homer games. Rodríguez is not rated well defensively but his bat could be an exciting addition to the Reds’ lineup. -- Mark Sheldon
NL West
D-backs: RHP Zac Gallen

When they re-signed Zac Gallen just after Spring Training started, the Diamondbacks were ecstatic at the reunion. Gallen, though, hasn't been able to put together full outings and recently landed on the injured list with elbow inflammation. With starters Corbin Burnes and Ryne Nelson out until September, getting Gallen healthy and pitching like his old self is a priority for the rotation. -- Steve Gilbert
Dodgers: RF Kyle Tucker

Tucker has fallen short of the expectations that came with his four-year, $240 million contract, although he seemed to be gaining some momentum heading into the All-Star break. The Dodgers were one of the highest-scoring teams in baseball in the first half, but even so, the offensive production has been inconsistent for much of the season. Having Tucker perform closer to his capabilities in the heart of the order would considerably lengthen the lineup. -- Sonja Chen
Giants: 1B/DH Bryce Eldridge

Eldridge has made it clear that he wants to develop into the face of the franchise, so his development will continue to be a key storyline coming out of the All-Star break. The 21-year-old slugger possesses game-changing power and has looked comfortable handing first base thus far, so it wouldn’t be a surprise to see him force his way into the conversation for National League Rookie of the Year if he puts up big numbers in the second half. -- Maria Guardado
Padres: RHP Nick Pivetta

Let’s say the Padres add a pitcher or two at the Deadline. Let’s say they close the gap in the Wild Card race and find themselves in contention. The problems in their starting rotation could disappear when Pivetta returns from his elbow injury (and Joe Musgrove after that). Then again, elbow injuries are tricky. Who knows what version of Pivetta the Padres would be getting? If they get the same pitcher as last year, it could set them up nicely for a late run (and, potentially, for a playoff series). -- AJ Cassavell
Rockies: SS Ezequiel Tovar

After a rousing performance for World Baseball Classic champion Venezuela, shortstop Ezequiel Tovar was expected to move into the stardom that has been predicted for him. But he hasn’t found any consistency and hit the All-Star break with a -0.6 WAR per Baseball-Reference. Signed through 2030 with a club option for 2031, Tovar needs to catch fire to justify the trust that ownership has shown in him. -- Thomas Harding
items.titleZh: 下半赛季各队关键球员
bodyZh: MLB赛季下半程通常伴随着一项使命。对于竞争者来说，是进入季后赛。对于其他球队来说，是盘点和展望，为下赛季做准备。
无论哪种情况，球队都希望看到某些球员做出贡献，因为他们无论如何都将是未来重要的一部分。在MLB.com随队记者的帮助下，以下是每支球队在下半赛季的关键球员。
切换到：美联东区 | 美联中区 | 美联西区 | 国联东区 | 国联中区 | 国联西区
美联东区
蓝鸟：一垒手 Vladimir Guerrero Jr.
这整个赛季都没有改变。如果Vladdy能恢复到2025年季后赛时那个自己的75%，蓝鸟将打开无限可能。如果这没有发生，他们就需要四到五名其他打者同时火热。这支球队中没有球员比Vladdy更有能力改变蓝鸟的轨迹，但等待已经持续了近100场比赛。-- Keegan Matheson
金莺：游击手 Gunnar Henderson
毫无疑问，是Henderson。这位25岁的明星游击手本赛季表现挣扎，96场比赛打击率.224，OPS .697。他的力量仍在（17支本垒打，排名球队第二），但表现水平较其MLB生涯前三个完整赛季有所下降。不过Henderson可能正在扭转局面，上周末三场横扫皇家的比赛中，他13支5（.385），周日以三安打表现结束系列赛。如果他能保持下去，对金莺未来将是巨大推动。-- Jake Rill
光芒：左外野手 Chandler Simpson

Simpson不一定是光芒最重要的球员。他们的打线更依赖Yandy Díaz、Jonathan Aranda和Junior Caminero。很难想象没有Drew Rasmussen、Shane McClanahan、Nick Martinez或现在的Griffin Jax的先发轮值。没有Bryan Baker和Kevin Kelly，牛棚也会陷入困境。但Simpson是一张王牌，是打线从尚可到危险的区别。当他打出高打击率、让防守恐慌并给跑垒带来破坏时，光芒是另一支球队。-- Adam Berry
红袜：外野手 Roman Anthony 和左投手 Garrett Crochet
虽然球队在16胜2负的热潮中阵容齐整，但红袜最想念的球员是Anthony，他自5月4日以来因右手/手腕肌腱部分撕裂一直没有出场。如果Anthony能很快好转并在最后六周左右回归，将对一支本赛季大部分时间依赖投球的波士顿球队产生重大影响。而投球表现一直出色，受伤的王牌Crochet的回归——即使要到9月——也能给波士顿带来巨大提振。-- Ian Browne
洋基：外野手 Aaron Judge
从现在到赛季结束，洋基能做到的任何补强都比不上Aaron Judge健康回归。队长兼三次MVP自5月底以来一直未出战，进攻在他缺席期间长时间停滞。Judge在全明星期间重新接受了骨折右肋骨的影像检查，但他需要更多时间恢复。最佳情况似乎是Judge在8月某个时候回归，但也不要排除9月。-- Bryan Hoch
美联中区
守护者：三垒手 José Ramírez
毫不意外。Ramírez在手术后康复中已进入打击进阶阶段，可能不久就会开始康复任务。尽管克利夫兰在没有这位33岁球员的情况下表现不错，但他们急切等待他的回归。有了这位超级巨星三垒手居中打线，守护者的阵容水平更高。-- Tim Stebbins
皇家：一垒手 Vinnie Pasquantino
Pasquantino的上半赛季糟糕透顶，他会第一个承认这一点。他不仅在打线中间很难产出力量，整体产出也很挣扎，然后他在回归前处理了钩骨伤势。如果他在今年下半程能更像2025年的自己，将给皇家对他未来角色的信心。-- Anne Rogers
老虎：二垒手 Gleyber Torres
当Torres在6月2日从左腹斜肌拉伤中回归时，老虎的进攻看起来更危险，但15天后伤势加重再次进入伤病名单。他的本垒板纪律和接触能力使他自然适合担任第一棒，并加深了身后的打线，同时给打线提供了急需的对抗左投的右手老将。他本周开始康复任务，可能在全明星后不久回归。-- Jason Beck
双城：捕手 Ryan Jeffers
很多事情围绕Jeffers，他自信在因伤缺席一个半月后重新启动时会恢复状态。但你永远不知道打者从手腕伤势中回归会怎样。如果他能恢复受伤前的全明星级状态，将使已经强大的进攻达到新高度。-- Matthew Leach
白袜：一垒手 村上宗隆
简单来说，这位来自日本的强力一垒手以许多积极方式改变了球队。村上在全明星前因右腿筋二级拉伤缺席了35场比赛，白袜在他缺席期间取得17胜18负。他上周末回归时11支1并吞下7次三振，但白袜也轻松横扫了运动家三场。他的存在本身就帮助了打线，并带来更大的胜利感。-- Scott Merkin
美联西区
天使：三垒手 Denzer Guzman
年仅22岁，Guzman在三垒获得了长期观察，并展现了一些巨大潜力的闪光。他是天生的游击手，但在三垒表现出色，打击也能自立。27场比赛打击率.262/.307/.411，4支本垒打、4支二垒安打和14分打点，他的发展对球队至关重要。-- Rhett Bollinger
太空人：外野手 Brice Matthews
太空人进入周日时外野手OPS排名大联盟倒数第三。球队正在寻找任何能在左外野或中外野站稳脚跟的人，而Matthews一直在这些位置分配时间。Matthews已成为防守出色、臂力强劲的外野手，但如果他能打击再进一步，特别是在Daikin Park，进攻会看起来更好。-- Brian McTaggart
运动家：一垒手 Nick Kurtz
即使在他进入伤病名单前陷入低潮，Kurtz也打出了MVP级别的赛季。他是那种可以扛起球队的年轻球星，运动家需要他这样做来维持逐渐消失的季后赛希望。如果Kurtz缺席时间延长，运动家可能别无选择，只能成为卖家。-- Martín Gallegos
水手：中外野手 Julio Rodríguez
下半赛季的Julio是联盟中最好的球员之一。自2022年新秀赛季以来，他在全明星后合格打者中OPS排名第13（.902），本垒打排名第8（59）。更大的担忧是他如何从脑震荡中恢复，这一伤病使他在上半赛季最后九场缺席，以及他还可能缺席多久。-- Daniel Kramer
游骑兵：游击手 Corey Seager 和左外野手 Wyatt Langford
游骑兵最好的两名打者Seager（背部、脑震荡）和Langford（前臂、腿筋）本赛季总共只一起打了25场比赛。这可不是成功的秘诀。游骑兵在没有他们的情况下表现不错，保持在美联西区激烈竞争中，但如果想真正冲击，需要超级明星打出超级明星水平。
“你在春训中梦想的那些家伙，比如全部在一起看起来会怎样，我们就是没有过，”经理Skip Schumaker说。“Corey和Wyatt，一起，我不知道他们一起打了多少比例的比赛，但肯定不多。这肯定是挑战。其他填补的球员值得很多赞誉。我们一直在很好地生存——老实说比生存更好——直到他们回来。我认为我们在这方面做得非常出色。”-- Kennedi Landry
国联东区
勇士：三垒手 Austin Riley
Riley在2023年打出连续第三个30轰赛季时看起来像是一位常年MVP候选人。但他在此后三年中打出.719 OPS，总计44支本垒打。伤病（2024年骨折的手和2025年腹部拉伤）阻碍了他。但今年他健康，仍以.618 OPS进入全明星。下半程的反弹可以显著改善亚特兰大的打线。-- Mark Bowman
马林鱼：右投手 Anthony Bender 和左投手 John King
马林鱼将依赖Sandy Alcantara、全明星Max Meyer和Eury Pérez的三巨头轮值，以及二游组合Otto Lopez和二垒手Xavier Edwards。然而，对球队成功最重要的可能是受伤的后援投手Bender和King的回归，以帮助强大但吃紧的牛棚。Bender是球队的主要布局投手，而King被证明是国家联盟最可靠的左投之一。-- Christina De Nicola
大都会：右投手 Nolan McLean
即使在一个令人失望的赛季中，McLean也展现了那种使他成为今年国联新秀奖热门人选的前线潜力。尽管McLean对其武器库的控制一直可疑，但他进入全明星时控球更好，过去八次先发自责分率2.35。他的持续进步将使大都会在进入2027年时走上更好的道路。-- Anthony DiComo
国民：外野手 James Wood
23岁的Wood是驱动国民进攻的引擎。他已经在全明星前创下了单赛季最多首打席本垒打（10支）的球队纪录。这位6英尺6英寸、两届全明星在得分（89）和保送（79）上领先联盟，上垒率（.410）、长打率（.575）和OPS（.985）在国联排名第一。Wood有能力一棒改变国民比赛的走向。-- Jessica Camerato
费城人：右投手 Brad Keller、左投手 José Alvarado 或右投手 Orion Kerkering？
是Keller、Alvarado，甚至是Kerkering。费城人需要从他们的后段局数后援投手身上看到更多，如果他们想避免十月更多毁灭性的后段局数失利。经理Don Mattingly表示他对牛棚中的“额外”球员感觉良好，特别是如果轮值在下半程投得好。-- Todd Zolecki
国联中区
酿酒人：右投手 Jacob Misiorowski
酿酒人从未有过像他这样的投手。也许从未有人有过像他这样的投手。而密尔沃基作为世界大赛竞争者的一切理由都围绕将这位24岁、在ERA、三振、WHIP和对手打击率上领先大联盟的球员以全力状态送入季后赛。这将在未来几个月需要一些创造力，从预期在全明星和下半程开始的延长休息开始。酿酒人必须在Misiorowski周围有足够的投手来管理他的工作量，直至十月。-- Adam McCalvy
红雀：游击手 Masyn Winn
他的防守仍是白金手套级别，但他在大联盟的三个完整赛季中打击逐年下滑。他在6月和7月初打击有所进步；如果他能回到联盟平均打者水平，他将是一位全明星级球员，可能成为球队围绕建设的下一个球员。-- Will Leitch
小熊：右投手 Daniel Palencia
小熊今年对终结者Daniel Palencia寄予厚望，但伤病限制了这位火球右投手至今仅出赛19场，投了16又2/3局。牛棚因此缺乏稳定的终结点，证据是有10名投手至少取得一次救援成功。Palencia在'25年是小熊的终结者力量，然后在季后赛中是影响多局救火员。他还在世界棒球经典赛上为委内瑞拉出战。让他为冲刺阶段恢复健康可能对牛棚产生奇效。-- Jordan Bastian
海盗：外野手 Oneil Cruz
在因左手骨折缺席一个多月之前，Cruz有望打出历史性的40轰60盗赛季。只有Ronald Acuña Jr.和Shohei Ohtani完成过此成就。在他最好的赛季里，Cruz预计在全明星后不久从伤病名单回归。海盗已经有了大联盟顶级的进攻，Cruz只会火上浇油。-- Aiden Stepansky
红人：外野手 Héctor Rodríguez
辛辛那提排名第五的潜力新秀Rodríguez可能是下半程某个时候下一个大联盟首秀的球员。这位左打重炮手是在2022年交易截止日前从大都会组织交易Tyler Naquin得到的，在89场比赛中打出23支本垒打，包括一场三轰和三场两轰比赛。Rodríguez防守评价不高，但他的球棒可能成为红人打线令人兴奋的补充。-- Mark Sheldon
国联西区
响尾蛇：右投手 Zac Gallen
当他们在春训开始后不久重新签下Zac Gallen时，响尾蛇对重聚欣喜若狂。但Gallen一直无法完成完整出赛，最近因肘部炎症进入伤病名单。由于先发投手Corbin Burnes和Ryne Nelson要到9月才能回归，让Gallen恢复健康并像过去一样投球是轮值的优先事项。-- Steve Gilbert
道奇：右外野手 Kyle Tucker
Tucker未能达到他四年2.4亿美元合同带来的期望，尽管他在全明星前似乎获得了一些势头。道奇是上半赛季大联盟得分最高的球队之一，即便如此，进攻产出在本赛季大部分时间也不稳定。让Tucker在打线心脏位置更接近其能力发挥将大大延长打线。-- Sonja Chen
巨人：一垒手/指定打击 Bryce Eldridge
Eldridge已明确表示他希望成为球队门面，因此他的发展将继续是全明星后关键故事线。这位21岁的重炮手拥有改变比赛的力量，并且至今处理一垒看起来自在，因此如果他在下半程打出大数字，看到他进入国联最佳新秀的讨论不会令人惊讶。-- Maria Guardado
教士：右投手 Nick Pivetta
假设教士在交易截止日增加一两名投手。假设他们缩小外卡差距并发现自己处于竞争之中。当他们从肘伤中回归（以及之后的Joe Musgrove），他们先发轮值的问题可能会消失。但话说回来，肘伤是棘手的。谁知道教士会得到什么版本的Pivetta？如果他们得到去年同样的投手，可能为后期冲刺（以及潜在的季后赛系列）奠定良好基础。-- AJ Cassavell
洛矶：游击手 Ezequiel Tovar
在为世界棒球经典赛冠军委内瑞拉队表现出色后，游击手Ezequiel Tovar预期将迈向预测中的明星地位。但他一直没有找到一致性，以Baseball-Reference的-0.6 WAR进入全明星。合同签至2030年并包含2031年球队选项，Tovar需要火热表现来证明老板对他的信任。-- Thomas Harding
items.author: MLB.com

items.titleEn: Barger, Scherzer among Blue Jays waiting for good news on injury front
bodyEn: TORONTO -- It must have felt so strange for manager John Schneider to manage the All-Star Game without reading off a laundry list of injury updates a few hours before first pitch. Friday at Rogers Centre, he was back to business.

The Blue Jays need all the help they can get to climb out of this hole, but their return from the break brought more bad news than good, particularly with Addison Barger (back, right elbow), who has still not started any level of rehab activity.
This is what you need to know, and how it could impact the coming weeks.
The latest injury updates

RF Addison BargerWith no level of activity yet, this is getting worrying for Barger, who represented such upside this season but has only taken 22 at-bats. Both his right elbow injury from earlier in the year and Barger’s stress fracture in his back are still issues, so this isn’t a simple process. He’ll need weeks to build back up, almost like his own shortened Spring Training at this point, so the Blue Jays need to see some movement here by early August.
“I think we’re getting to the point where we either say ‘go’ or ‘no’,” Schneider said. “Dealing with those two things is tough. He’s frustrated. It will be August at the earliest."

RHP Max ScherzerWe’re back to the thumb issue with Scherzer. Over the break, he got three injections in that problematic right thumb. It’s not common to hear of a player getting three injections in the same spot, but now that Scherzer is past his back, left ankle and right forearm issues, this is the last one standing in his way. All of these injuries add up to Scherzer pitching just 22 innings this season, though, with a 10.23 ERA.

“I wouldn’t call it a step back, I would call it a bit of a pause,” Schneider said. “Everything else is feeling good -- arm, ankle, back. We’re trying to knock out all of the stuff around it. He felt this a little bit when he was throwing before the break back here, so we want to knock that out. Then, when we hit ‘go,' he can go and not have these stalls in between.”

OF Anthony SantanderJust as Santander was about to start swinging the bat again after his left shoulder surgery earlier this year, he felt something in that shoulder and has received a cortisone injection. This is yet another step back for Santander, whose first two seasons have been very difficult, with just 55 games played around these shoulder issues. At this point, it’s beginning to feel unlikely that we will see Santander in 2026.
What this means for the Blue Jays down the stretch

The Trade Deadline is important here, looming just over two weeks away on Aug. 3. The pending returns of outfielder Jesús Sánchez -- who is getting closer -- and reliever Yimi García could help, but these updates leave the Blue Jays even more reliant on their own players turning this around.

Barger, in particular, felt like someone who could be the saviour. He was simply spectacular in the 2025 postseason, batting .367 with a 1.024 OPS. It looked like something had been unlocked for Barger, who has always been so gifted physically, but was beginning to see the game slow down around him. Now, the Blue Jays need to plan as if both Barger and Santander will not be a factor in 2026. If either is, consider it a bonus.

In Scherzer’s case, he was never expected to be the saviour, but the Blue Jays hoped he could at least return in time to provide depth. With five “traditional” starters, the Blue Jays could shift Spencer Miles back into the bullpen full-time, where he remains extremely valuable, but this rotation remains a four-man show with Miles as the swingman. That puts the Blue Jays one injury away from a dangerous spot, too.

This season has been defined by bad news, too often the type of news that comes three hours before first pitch when Schneider reads out the injury updates. Friday’s return to play didn’t bring much hope, so if it wasn’t already clear to everyone, the fate of the 2026 season rests in the hands of the 26 players in that clubhouse.
items.titleZh: 巴杰、薛泽等蓝鸟球员伤病待好转
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟伤病报告：巴杰尚无康复活动，薛泽拇指注射三针，桑坦德肩膀注射可的松。
bodyZh: 多伦多——对于总教练John Schneider来说，在没有于赛前几小时列出一长串伤病更新消息的情况下执教全明星赛，一定感觉非常奇怪。周五在罗杰斯中心，他又回到了日常工作中。
蓝鸟需要一切可能的帮助来摆脱困境，但他们全明星周末后的回归带来了更多坏消息而非好消息，尤其是Addison Barger（背部、右肘）仍未开始任何级别的康复活动。
以下是你需要了解的情况，以及这可能对未来几周产生的影响。
最新伤病更新
右外野手Addison Barger：目前尚无任何活动，这让巴杰的情况令人担忧。他本季原本展现出巨大潜力，却仅获得22个打席。早前的右肘伤势以及背部的应力性骨折仍是问题，因此这不是一个简单的过程。他需要数周时间恢复重建，几乎就像他现在进行一个缩短版的春训。因此，蓝鸟需要在8月初之前看到一些进展。
“我认为我们正接近要么说‘行’要么说‘不行’的地步，”Schneider说道。“同时处理这两个问题很艰难。他很沮丧。最早也要到8月了。”
右投手Max Scherzer：又回到薛泽的拇指问题。全明星周末期间，他的问题右拇指接受了三次注射。很少听说一名球员在同一位置接受三次注射，但现在薛泽已经解决了背部、左脚踝和右前臂的问题，这是最后一个阻碍。所有这些伤病使得薛泽本季仅投了22局，自责分率高达10.23。
“我不会称之为倒退，我会称之为暂停，”Schneider说道。“其他部位感觉都很好——手臂、脚踝、背部。我们正在努力解决周围的所有问题。全明星周末前他在这里投球时就有点感觉，所以我们想解决它。然后，当我们说‘开始’时，他就能继续前进，中间不会有这些停顿。”
外野手Anthony Santander：就在桑坦德今年早些时候左肩手术后即将重新开始挥棒时，他感到肩膀有些不适，并接受了可的松注射。这对桑坦德来说是又一次倒退，他的前两个赛季非常艰难，因肩部问题只打了55场比赛。此时，越来越可能我们在2026年看不到桑坦德了。
这对蓝鸟近期的意义
交易截止日很关键，距离8月3日不到两周的时间。外野手Jesús Sánchez（接近回归）和后援投手Yimi García的回归可能有所帮助，但这些消息让蓝鸟更加依赖自身球员扭转局面。
特别是Barger，他感觉像是能成为救世主的人。他在2025年季后赛中表现惊艳，打击率.367，OPS 1.024。似乎巴杰找到了破解之道，他一直以来都极具天赋，但现在开始看到比赛节奏在他周围放慢。现在，蓝鸟需要做计划时假定巴杰和桑坦德在2026年都不会有贡献。如果其中一人能回归，那就视为奖励。
至于薛泽，大家从未期望他是救世主，但蓝鸟希望他至少能及时回归提供深度。有了五位“传统”先发，蓝鸟可以将Spencer Miles完全移回牛棚，他在那里依然极具价值，但先发轮值现在仍是四人搭配Miles这位长中继。这也让蓝鸟距离危险境地只差一个伤病。
本季一直被坏消息所定义，而且往往是Schneider在赛前三小时念伤病更新时那种坏消息。周五的复赛并未带来太多希望，因此如果之前还不清楚，那么2026赛季的命运就掌握在那26名球员手中了。
items.author: Keegan Matheson

items.titleEn: Blue Jays, top pick Carlon agree to deal just below slot value (source)
bodyEn: TORONTO -- The Blue Jays have agreed to a $2.4 million bonus with their first pick from the 2026 MLB Draft, lefty Cole Carlon.

The bonus, first reported by MLB Pipeline’s Jim Callis, comes in just below the slot value for the No. 39 pick, which is $2,571,700. The Blue Jays did not pick in the first round because they, along with the Dodgers, Mets, Phillies and Yankees, received a 10-pick penalty on their first pick for exceeding the second surcharge threshold of the Competitive Balance Tax, which bumped them back to No. 39.

Carlon is a powerhouse on the mound, every bit of 6-foot-5, 230 pounds with the ability to touch triple digits. Callis also calls his slider the best in the MLB Draft, and the Blue Jays were thrilled to land the Arizona State product where they did, expecting him to be off the board by the time they got their first crack at the Draft.

“One of my friends texted me after, and I was laughing about the way he put it. He said that ‘He’s got two October pitches,’” said director of amateur scouting Marc Tramuta. “I felt like that was a good characterization of the fastball and slider that are currently in place. We just like the big physicality at 6-foot-5. We think there’s some athleticism to his delivery.”

Carlon’s 133 strikeouts over just 83 2/3 innings ranked fifth in Division 1 this past NCAA season, and given the Blue Jays’ success with Trey Yesavage coming out of college, it’s easy to dream on how quickly Carlon could climb the ladder.

Given his NCAA workload this season, which was already ramping up from a bullpen role in previous seasons, don’t expect the Blue Jays to push Carlon much more in 2026. It’s possible he’s shut down entirely and spends the rest of this summer focused on development at the club’s complex in Dunedin, Fla., but once Carlon gets on a mound, he’ll be a must-watch prospect in this system.

“He throws backwards. He throws his slider more than any other pitch,” said Baseball America’s Carlos Collazo on MLB Network’s Draft coverage. “He throws that about 52% of the time. You look at how big this guy is, I see Robbie Ray, just a bigger version of him. The Blue Jays had [Ray]. This guy is going to come at you, but you’re going to be guessing. Are you going to get a 100 mph pitch or are you going to get a slider that has incredible movement?”

Money matters, of course, especially with the Blue Jays having the second-smallest bonus pool in the Draft. Coming in roughly $170,000 below the slot value on Carlon’s pick is valuable, along with some of the bonus pool money the Blue Jays are expected to save in rounds four through 10, where they went heavy on college picks. All of these arrows point to catcher Will Brick, the high schooler who represents the Blue Jays’ “big swing” of this class.

Brick was widely considered the best prep catcher in this class and had been connected to the Blue Jays as early as the No. 39 pick, but they landed him in the fourth round (No. 131) and are expected to make an aggressive, over-slot play to land him. An excellent defensive catcher, Brick’s power also has Tramuta and the Blue Jays’ scouting group excited. Presuming the Blue Jays pull this off, which would be a major win for their farm system, Brick would immediately become one of the highest-upside position players in Toronto’s Top 30.
items.titleZh: 蓝鸟与首轮秀Carlon达成协议，奖金略低于标准值
items.summaryZh: 多伦多蓝鸟队与2026年选秀首轮选中投手Cole Carlon达成240万美元奖金协议。
bodyZh: 多伦多 -- 蓝鸟队已与他们在2026年MLB选秀中的首轮选中投手Cole Carlon达成240万美元奖金协议。
据MLB Pipeline的Jim Callis首先报道，该奖金略低于第39顺位的标准值2,571,700美元。蓝鸟队没有在首轮选秀，因为他们与道奇、大都会、费城人和洋基队一样，因超过竞争平衡税的第二级门槛而受到首轮选秀权后移10位的处罚，从而掉到第39顺位。
Carlon是投手丘上的重炮，身高6英尺5英寸（约1.96米），体重230磅（约104公斤），能投出三位数的球速。Callis还称他的滑球是本届MLB选秀中最好的，蓝鸟队很高兴能在这个位置得到亚利桑那州立大学的这位产品，原本预计他会在他们首次选秀前就被选中。
“选秀后我的一个朋友给我发信息，我对他的表述方式感到好笑。他说‘他有两种十月利器’，”业余球探总监Marc Tramuta说。“我觉得这是对他现有快速球和滑球的恰当描述。我们喜欢他6英尺5英寸的大体格。我们认为他的投球动作有一定的运动能力。”
在刚刚过去的NCAA赛季中，Carlon在83又2/3局中投出133次三振，在Division 1中排名第五。鉴于蓝鸟队让Trey Yesavage从大学出来后取得的成功，很容易想象Carlon能多快爬升梯队。
考虑到他本赛季在NCAA的投球量（已比之前赛季的牛棚角色有所增加），不要指望蓝鸟队在2026年让Carlon投更多。他有可能完全休整，并将这个夏天的剩余时间花在球队位于佛罗里达州达尼丁的训练基地的发展上，但一旦Carlon登上投手丘，他将是这个体系中值得关注的潜力新秀。
“他颠倒投球。他投滑球的次数超过其他任何球种，”《棒球美国》的Carlos Collazo在MLB Network的选秀报道中说。“他大约52%的时间投滑球。你看看这家伙有多大，我看到Robbie Ray，比他更大一号的版本。蓝鸟队曾经拥有[Ray]。这家伙会向你进攻，但你会猜测。你会面对100英里/小时的球还是有着不可思议位移的滑球？”
当然，金钱也很重要，尤其是蓝鸟队拥有选秀中第二小的奖金池。在Carlon的签位上比标准值节省约17万美元是很宝贵的，再加上蓝鸟队预计在第四轮到第十轮（他们大量选择大学球员）节省的一些奖金池资金。所有这些线索都指向捕手Will Brick，这位高中生代表了蓝鸟队本届选秀的“大赌注”。
Brick被广泛认为是本届选秀中最好的高中捕手，早在第39顺位就被与蓝鸟队联系起来，但他们最终在第四轮（第131顺位）选中了他，并预计会采取积极的溢价签约来争取他。作为一名出色的防守型捕手，Brick的力量也让Tramuta和蓝鸟队的球探团队兴奋。假设蓝鸟队成功签下他（这将是他们农场系统的一大胜利），Brick将立即成为多伦多前30名新秀中上限最高的进攻球员之一。
items.author: Keegan Matheson

items.titleEn: Each team's biggest trade chip as Deadline nears
bodyEn: You've got to give to get during Trade Deadline season.

We've already gone through each team's biggest Deadline need, but what do these clubs have to offer to address that need? With the help of our MLB.com beat reporters, here's a look at each club's biggest trade chip.
Jump to: AL East | AL Central | AL West | NL East | NL Central | NL West
AL EAST

BLUE JAYS: SS Arjun NimmalaThis isn’t a commentary on Nimmala himself, but the Blue Jays would only trade No. 1 prospect JoJo Parker in a franchise-changing deal, and Nimmala represents that next level of value, now ranked the No. 45 prospect in baseball while enjoying a strong season in Double-A. On the pitching front, Toronto could be hesitant to deal lefty Johnny King (No. 4), but righty Gage Stanifer (No. 6) could be an attractive name.

ORIOLES: One of their three Top 100 prospectsWould the Orioles consider trading any of their three Top 100 prospects (outfielder Nate George, outfielder/first baseman Ike Irish or left-hander Joseph Dzierwa)? They might have to move one if they want to make a huge splash. There are some other strong pieces in the farm system who could be viable chips, likely more on the pitching side. If Baltimore makes any seller-type moves, it would be most likely to trade players on expiring contracts, such as left-hander Trevor Rogers or outfielder Taylor Ward.

RAYS: Prospect depthThe Rays’ deep Minor League system has only gotten better thanks to a few breakout seasons and another Draft class full of high-upside players. Theo Gillen, Nathan Flewelling and Caden Bodine are Top 100 prospects, and No. 2 overall pick Grady Emerson will join Gillen atop Tampa Bay’s system. So, take a quick scroll down their Top 30 Prospects list -- or look at recently graduated prospects such as shortstop Carson Williams -- and you can see why the Rays are well-positioned to be aggressive at this Deadline. They don’t necessarily have to deal their top prospects, because they have so many they can use to make whatever moves needed.

RED SOX: Pitching prospectsThe Red Sox have built a lot of pitching depth in the farm system since chief baseball officer Craig Breslow, now in his third season, took over. While right-hander Anthony Eyanson, the team’s No. 2 prospect, might be close to untouchable, the Sox have plenty of other arms who could attract rebuilding teams, including Kyson Witherspoon and Marcus Phillips, two SEC righties who were taken ahead of Eyanson in the 2025 Draft.

YANKEES: RHP Elmer RodríguezWith the Yankees apparently unwilling to dangle George Lombard Jr. and Carlos Lagrange, Rodríguez could anchor a difference-making deal. The right-hander has made four big league starts this season, pitching to a 4.76 ERA across 17 innings. Rodríguez, 22, was the return in the Carlos Narváez trade and is ranked as the club’s No. 3 prospect and No. 74 overall by MLB Pipeline.
AL CENTRAL

GUARDIANS: INF Angel Genao (No. 2 prospect, No. 41 overall)The Guardians promoted Genao to Triple-A Columbus on May 4, but he doesn't have a clear path to the Majors. The 22-year-old is a shortstop who has experience playing second and third base. Cleveland has Brayan Rocchio, Travis Bazzana and José Ramírez occupying those positions. While there isn’t any rush to move Genao given the logjam, he could be a trade chip if the Guardians want to make a splash.

ROYALS: RHP Michael WachaThe All-Star starter would likely bring the biggest return, but the Royals must decide if they’re willing to part with him -- and how other teams value the 35-year-old is going to be a big part of that conversation. The Royals will also see what interest there is in lefty reliever Daniel Lynch IV, who has two more years of control and past starting experience.

TIGERS: LHP Tarik Skubal or RHP Casey MizeSkubal will be the biggest name in baseball around the Deadline, just as he has been since last offseason. But with just two months of control and free agency looming, dreams of a massive prospect package might be rosy -- unless the Dodgers decide to jump in and then extend him without needing to give up a Draft pick. If the Tigers indeed end up becoming sellers, Mize is a sneaky name to watch and could attract a wider market than Skubal. And like Skubal, Mize is headed for free agency at season’s end.
Trade rumors: Skubal suitors emerge, Yanks eyeing Mason Miller?

TWINS: Prospects or a Major League outfielder or RHP Joe RyanIt depends whether they buy or sell. The Twins have four Top 100 Prospects: Walker Jenkins, Emmanuel Rodriguez, Kaelen Culpepper and Eduardo Tait. They are highly unlikely to move Jenkins or Culpepper. If they sell, they’ll get tons of calls on Ryan and catcher Ryan Jeffers. It wouldn’t be shocking if the Twins moved someone such as outfielder Trevor Larnach to bolster their Major League pitching in a deal, clearing space for someone like Jenkins.

WHITE SOX: Prospects -- with a catchGeneral manager Chris Getz has meticulously rebuilt this organization, from the players to the infrastructure, and he certainly won’t tear things apart to jump up for a one-year possibility. Don’t look for shortstop Caleb Bonemer -- the No. 1 White Sox prospect and No. 16 overall -- or right fielder Braden Montgomery to be on the move. But to get something of value, Getz will have to give up something of future value.
AL WEST

ANGELS: LHP Reid DetmersShortstop Zach Neto is their most valuable chip, but he doesn’t seem likely to be moved because he’s under team control for three more years. However, Detmers is under control through next year and has premium stuff from the left side that is tantalizing for other clubs. He posted a 4.39 ERA in 19 starts in the first half but had strong peripheral stats with 123 strikeouts, 35 walks and 12 homers allowed in 108 2/3 innings.

ASTROS: RHP A.J. BlubaughWith a farm system that is still ranked near the bottom, the Astros may not have the kind of trade chips required to make a major acquisition, but that doesn’t mean they can’t add an impact player. It might take trading from the Major League roster. One player to keep an eye on is Blubaugh, a hard-throwing reliever who threw at least two innings in nine of his final 11 outings before the All-Star break.

ATHLETICS: 3B/1B Tommy WhiteAssuming they remain buyers, the A’s are desperate for an upgrade in the pitching department -- both starting and relieving -- and White, the club’s No. 7 prospect, would be an attractive chip as a Major League-ready player with elite hitting traits. If they sell, one has to wonder if catcher Shea Langeliers becomes available, though any selling would likely be on a lower level. One example would be trading a veteran reliever, such as Mark Leiter Jr., to a contender.

MARINERS: Starting rotationSources have said the Mariners are more open than they’ve ever been to dealing from their rotation, but among that group, their strongest preference would be to move Luis Castillo. He comes with complications, though, chiefly the $24.15 million he’s due next season and the $25 million vesting option he could achieve by pitching 180 innings in 2027. It could hypothetically force the Mariners to package Castillo with a big league hitter and prospect capital to offset the cost.

RANGERS: They don’t really have many leftThis is assuming the Rangers don’t sell and big dogs such as Corey Seager, Jacob deGrom and Nathan Eovaldi remain in Texas (though it would take a certifiable collapse for them to be on the trade block anyway). But dating back to the 2025 Trade Deadline, the Rangers have parted ways with a total of 11 prospects for Merrill Kelly, Danny Coulombe, Phil Maton and MacKenzie Gore. The farm system ranks in the bottom six, according to MLB Pipeline.

They have a pair of Top 100 prospects in Sebastian Walcott (No. 10) and Caden Scarborough (No. 63), but the depth of the farm system likely won’t net them any major names as it did in 2023, when they acquired Aroldis Chapman, Max Scherzer and Jordan Montgomery.
NL EAST

BRAVES: OF Eric HartmanHartman (the Braves' No. 2 prospect and No. 77 overall) might be the game’s most interesting trade chip this year. The 20-year-old outfielder has hit .281 with 21 homers, 31 stolen bases and a .902 OPS over 80 games for High-A Rome. The question: Should the Braves deal him now, with the hope his value will never get any higher? Or should they keep the young outfielder with the confidence he could soon become one of the game’s top superstars?

MARLINS: LHP Braxton GarrettThe Marlins may be hesitant to trade away a starting pitcher, considering how injuries already have impacted their depth, and with All-Star Max Meyer and Eury Pérez testing their previous workloads. Garrett was drafted by a previous front office and hasn't been recalled since mid-May despite pitching to a 1.54 ERA at Triple-A. Although Garrett struggled in two big league starts this year in his return from elbow surgery, he has a track record of success (4.16 career ERA) and postseason experience.

METS: RHP Freddy PeraltaDespite his struggles and despite the fact that he can become a free agent after this season, Peralta can still probably fetch as much as any tradeable player on the Mets’ roster. He has the pedigree of a two-time All-Star and is less than one year removed from finishing second in NL Cy Young Award voting. Given how much starting pitching is in demand around the league, the Mets would do well to explore Peralta’s market.

NATIONALS: SS CJ AbramsAbrams was a topic of trade buzz this past offseason because of his controllable contract, and his All-Star performance has boosted that value. Abrams, who doesn’t turn 26 until Oct. 3, was the National League's starting shortstop for the Midsummer Classic. He leads NL shortstops in home runs (20), RBIs (67) and slugging percentage (.510). While Abrams is the Nats’ biggest chip, All-Star southpaw Foster Griffin could be their most likely traded player, should the team decide to sell, because of his expiring contract.

PHILLIES: RHP Gage WoodIt’s definitely No. 2 prospect Wood, if teams are looking for a big-time talent who is healthy and close to big league ready. The Phillies’ first-round pick in the 2025 Draft is pitching for Double-A Reading. The Phillies are thin after Wood. Top prospect Aidan Miller remains sidelined with an injured back. The 22-year-old shortstop hasn’t played since last season, and there is no timetable for his return.
NL CENTRAL

BREWERS: SS Luis PeñaThe Brewers aren’t going to trade shortstop Cooper Pratt or outfielder Luis Lara after locking both into long-term contracts, and they aren’t going to trade the No. 1 prospect in baseball, Jesús Made. Considering how deep they are at shortstop in the organization, it could make sense for them to move Peña, a five-tool player who is baseball’s No. 18 prospect, per MLB Pipeline. Trouble is, Peña's value is down because of a health scare this season, when he collapsed in the dugout. He’s played 32 Minor League games this year.

CARDINALS: OF Lars NootbaarHe looks healthier than he has in years -- to the point that he can even play center field -- and he is under team control through 2027. His metrics have long pointed to better results than he has sometimes seen on the field; another team would love to be the one to unlock him.

CUBS: INF/OF Matt ShawShaw does not have a consistent home on the field for the Cubs, but he is a former first-rounder (2023) who is still just 24 years old and has a solid early track record in the Majors. With Nico Hoerner, Dansby Swanson and Alex Bregman all locked into Chicago’s infield spots, Shaw has worked as a super sub (mostly in the outfield) in 2026. While the Cubs are not looking to trade him, teams with a need on the infield will surely ask about Shaw’s availability.

PIRATES: 1B Spencer HorwitzThis one might come as a surprise. While the Pirates have plenty of prospects to deal, they could dig into their big league roster to acquire more pitching. Horwitz is under club control through 2030, and with the emergence of Esmerlyn Valdez in right field, Ryan O'Hearn could be better suited for first base. That would make Horwitz, who could return from a left hamstring strain this month, an intriguing chip with great value.
