老虎：右打或投手新秀。如果老虎要全力冲击，一位经验丰富的右打是经理A.J. Hinch利用有效代打和擅长打击左投的角色球员来取得对阵优势的必需品。作为一支球队，他们对左投的.714 OPS相比去年的.774（排名大联盟第二）有所下滑。现在，在Jahmai Jones被指定让渡后，新秀Hao-Yu Lee、Ben Malgeri和Eduardo Valencia被要求承担重任。另一方面，如果老虎决定出售，他们需要为农场系统补充投手。即使首轮选中了Cameron Flukey，底特律仍然缺乏有前景的年轻大联盟先发。
双城：后援投手。就是牛棚。即使引进了Tommy Nance——而且Andrew Morris和Yoendrys Gomez已经成为比赛末段可靠的组合——双城牛棚仍然太浅。他们应该很快就能迎回Cole Sands，但他们至少需要再增加一到两名可靠的后援投手。他们还需要另一名先发吗？可能。加强防守？当然。但后援补强是第一、第二和第三优先级。
白袜：投手。他们的系统中已有可用资源，比如牛棚的右投Tanner McDougal、开季先发Shane Smith作为轮值选项，以及左投Hagen Smith扮演介于两者之间的角色。但随着一些大联盟年轻投手接近投球局数新高，增加能够提供优质深度的球员将比引进一名主力投手更重要。从竞争者到非竞争者，所有人都想要投手，因此对总经理Chris Getz来说，获取成本可能很高。
美联西区
天使：增加年轻野手。天使农场系统投手众多，因此如果他们交易掉有价值的球员如José Soriano或Reid Detmers，尝试获取有多年控制权的野手是有意义的。他们在进攻端需要一切可能的帮助，尤其是在外野。寻找注重联系性的击球员也是理想之选。
太空人：左打外野手。增加一名左打外野手将是太空人的首要目标。休斯顿整个赛季外野产出不佳，尤其是左外野和中外野。Lamonte Wade Jr.在健康时稍微填补了空缺，但休斯顿需要升级Taylor Trammell、Brice Matthews和Jake Meyers的组合。
运动家：投手。运动家急需投手，5.21的自责分率排名大联盟第二差，仅好于洛矶。作为买家，他们可能会瞄准一位经验丰富的先发和一位老将后援。作为卖家，任何交易也都会着眼于获取投手以用于未来。
水手：右打、经验丰富的后援。西雅图面对左投的OPS为.631，大联盟最差，而他们整个内野都是左打更是雪上加霜。坦率地说，改进也必须来自内部——最明显的是Cal Raleigh，他对左投的OPS仅为.441，只有一支本垒打。水手显然也需要至少一名牛棚补强。虽然他们预计Matt Brash将在8月中旬回归，但他们不愿完全依赖一名长期伤缺后归来的球员。

游骑兵：另一位高杠杆投手。当然，先发投手永远不嫌多。但游骑兵有望迎来轮值增援，Jordan Montgomery即将回归，Cody Bradford也不远了。如果一切顺利，Jack Leiter甚至会在赛季末前回归。但牛棚的情况则不同。虽然Jacob Latz在终结者角色上表现出色——甚至首次入选全明星——而Jakob Junis在布局角色上也同样出色，但另一位高杠杆投手可以让游骑兵在关键时刻走得更远。偏好右投手。
国联东区
勇士：先发投手。Chris Sale目前是球队唯一具备季后赛水准的先发投手。你可以轻松论证这支球队需要两名先发投手，其中一位是前段轮值投手。在Jurickson Profar被禁赛后，勇士获得了额外的1500万美元可花费，而农场系统自2016年以来从未如此强大。因此，预计勇士将非常积极和激进地追求先发投手，也可能追求一位能加强指定打击位置的击球员。
马林鱼：右打。马林鱼使用分工制，由于阵容左打过多，这有时对他们不利。上半赛季，迈阿密在左投先发的比赛中战绩仅为10胜15负（大联盟胜率并列第七差），场均仅得3.8分（并列第三少）。随着Connor Norby和Graham Pauley在3A，Javier Sanoja和Leo Jiménez分担三垒上场时间，这正是一个用右打升级的绝佳位置。
大都会：年轻先发投手。哪里有价值，大都会就必须通过交易来挖掘。特别是，纽约的农场系统需要更稳定地培养出大联盟质量的先发投手。如果他们能获得那些最快在2027年就能产生影响的投手新秀，那将是理想之选。已经在大联盟的年轻先发也会对他们有帮助。
国民：牛棚可靠性。国民进入全明星休赛期，以联盟最高的27次救援失败告终。他们的牛棚自责分率国联最高（5.07）。在全明星休赛期前的最后一个系列赛中，国民在客场被洋基横扫，三次在比赛后期被逆转。有十名投手记录过救援成功，球队根据对阵情况调度投手，牛棚经历了大量阵容调整以补充投手。
费城人：一位比赛后期、高杠杆的后援投手。费城人球迷可能会认为球队最大的需求是一名右打，因为自2023年国联冠军赛第二战以来，球队得分不足。但牛棚在过去三个十月份也都表现不佳，从目前情况来看，他们绝对需要至少再增加一位比赛后期、高杠杆的后援投手，以填补轮值与终结者Jhoan Duran之间的空白。
国联中区

酿酒人：投球局数。鉴于Brandon Woodruff的肩膀和Kyle Harrison的肘部存在不确定性，而且Jacob Misiorowski已经接近去年（小联盟、大联盟和季后赛）的总局数（相差31局），酿酒人可能需要增加先发投手深度。但除2008年的CC Sabathia这一显著例外，他们几乎从未在交易截止日孤注一掷，而是更倾向于围绕为了获得高知名度的租将所需的年轻新秀来建队。今年他们会全力出击吗？
红雀：一位能吃局数的先发投手。许多人一直在等待红雀先发投手群崩溃，但它仍然撑住了，尽管三振率排名大联盟倒数第三。但正如全明星赛前不久在对阵酿酒人的一场残酷主场系列赛中所见，圣路易斯没有容错空间。另一名先发投手会提供一些缓冲，并减轻日益过度使用的牛棚的压力。而如果红雀最终决定出售，年轻投手将成为全面重点。
小熊：全面投手补充。经验丰富的野手团队相当固定和锁定——无论是角色还是合同——所以增加投手一直是重点。这一点在2026赛季中愈发清晰。4月Cade Horton因肘部手术赛季报销是轮值遭受的众多打击中的第一个，牛棚也遭遇了一长串伤病挫折。北边人将在未来几周内寻求在这两方面进行补强。
海盗：高杠杆后援投手。即使在上一周末选秀中用第34顺位选秀权从白袜交易得到左投Brandon Eisert，海盗仍然需要牛棚补强。一旦Evan Sisk从伤病名单回归，Eisert将为匹兹堡提供四名可靠的左投手。海盗渴望可靠的右投手来投后期局，尤其是因为本季初担任球队终结者的Dennis Santana稳定性不足。
红人：受球队控制的击球员。如果红人真的出售——除了那些进入合同年球员——他们应该换回仍有多年球队控制权的大联盟球员，而不仅仅是新秀。辛辛那提尤其需要更多进攻火力。例如，红人上赛季休赛期以自由球员身份签下外野手JJ Bleday时，部分考虑就是他通过2028年都受球队控制。
国联西区
响尾蛇：一垒手。如果响尾蛇是买家，他们需要升级的一个明确位置是一垒。在Ildemaro Vargas四月份打出职业生涯最佳一个月后，这个位置成为了真正的弱点。Pavin Smith受伤病困扰，回归后表现挣扎，被指定让渡。休赛期签下一年期合同的Carlos Santana，上场时间很少就受伤，也被DFA了。目前，Vargas和Tim Tawa在那里分担上场时间，亚利桑那渴望任何形式的产出。
道奇：轮值补强。这可能来自内部，因为道奇有Blake Snell（左肘游离体移除手术）和Tyler Glasnow（下背部痉挛）正在康复中。在理想情况下，Snell将在8月中旬回归轮值，Glasnow大约在月底跟上。如果其中任何一位先发出现问题，则可能在截止日前需要寻求外部轮值帮助。
巨人：可控的投手。巨人本赛季投手群产出不稳定，因此瞄准能提升2026年及以后球队实力的年轻投手对他们有好处。寻找可靠的后援投手是当前更迫切的需求，因为旧金山休赛期并未大力投资牛棚，今年在比赛后期难以守住领先。
教士：先发投手。圣迭戈需要多少先发投手？这取决于几个因素。首先也是最重要的，教士需要弄清楚他们能期待从Nick Pivetta和Joe Musgrove身上得到什么，两人预计都在8月从肘部伤病中回归。他们还需要评估轮值其他成员。Michael King是球队的中流砥柱，类似于王牌。除此之外？只有伤病和问号。一名先发投手可能不够。

洛矶：全面天赋，尤其是先发投手。冬季期间，洛矶收集了老将先发（Michael Lorenzen、Tomoyuki Sugano和Jose Quintana，加上左投Kyle Freeland），但对成熟先发的需求仍然存在。洛矶目前轮值中有右投Tanner Gordon和头号新秀第16位的Gabriel Hughes，以及第11号新秀Sean Sullivan在大联盟轮值和3A之间来回，但他们可以增加大联盟即战力先发投手。此外，要拥有真正稳定的进攻，他们需要在打线顶端和中心增加击球员——无论是成熟球员还是接近大联盟的新秀。
items.author: MLB.com

items.titleEn: Blue Jays play key roles in AL's shutout win at 2026 All-Star Game
bodyEn: In an All-Star Game that celebrated America’s 250th birthday in the city that’s considered the country’s birthplace, it was the American League pitchers who shined. They combined to allow just three hits and no extra-base hits while striking out 15 in a 4-0 shutout at Philadelphia’s Citizens Bank Park on Tuesday.

After the AL had won nine straight Midsummer Classics from 2013-22, the National League had won two of the last three editions, but 11 pitchers combined to shut down the NL in the game’s first shutout in 13 years.

The AL got out to an early lead with three runs against hometown Phillies starter Cristopher Sánchez in the first, keyed by a pair of Yankees with Cody Bellinger ripping a two-run single and Ben Rice adding an RBI single, before the White Sox’s Miguel Vargas crushed a solo blast in the eighth. Bellinger earned MVP honors for driving in the game’s first two runs.
From a night dominated by pitching, here’s a player-by-player look at the 2026 MLB All-Star Game:
Jump to: AL East | AL Central | AL West | NL East | NL Central | NL West

Blue JaysRHP Dylan Cease: The fourth Blue Jays pitcher to ever start the Midsummer Classic, Cease struck out Kyle Schwarber, Juan Soto and CJ Abrams in a 25-pitch first inning, working around a two-out walk to Freddie Freeman. Fans got to hear Cease talk through his pitch-selection process with catcher Shea Langeliers, as the battery was mic’d up the whole inning.

2B Ernie Clement: The AL’s leading vote-getter went hitless in two at-bats from the No. 9 spot in the order, but he made a highlight-reel play at second base -- against the Dodger who pulled off a defensive gem against him in Game 7 of last year’s World Series. With two outs and nobody on in the fifth, Clement quickly ranged up the middle to stop Andy Pages’ ground ball, then made a strong leaping throw to retire Pages, who (understandably) seemed to think he was getting a single off Rays right-hander Nick Martinez.

1B Vladimir Guerrero Jr: Voted to start in his sixth All-Star Game despite an underwhelming first half, Guerrero declined the opportunity to play and instead went home to Tampa, Fla., to rest his sore lower back.

RHP Louis Varland: Working the bottom of the eighth, Varland came out hot. His first pitch was a 100.2 mph sinker to Nationals slugger James Wood, although it was a ball. Varland caught Wood looking at a curveball for strike three, gave up a single to Pete Crow-Armstrong, then picked up two more outs to complete an 11-pitch inning.

OriolesC Adley Rutschman: Perhaps it’s fitting that Rutschman, playing arguably the best defense of his career, entered his third career All-Star Game as a defensive replacement in the seventh inning. The Orioles’ star catcher caught a hitless seventh inning, grounded out to lead off the eighth, then worked the rest of the AL’s combined shutout behind the plate. He experienced two batterymate reunions, too: one with former Oregon State teammate Drew Rasmussen, then another with former Orioles reliever Bryan Baker.

RaysRHP Bryan Baker: The Rays’ latest pitching success story was summoned from the AL bullpen to get the final out of the ninth inning, and he did exactly that. After giving up a single to Otto Lopez, Baker got Sal Stewart to hit a soft comebacker, fielded it cleanly and fired a throw to Munetaka Murakami to seal the AL’s shutout. Then he got to celebrate with Rutschman, his former Orioles teammate.

3B Junior Caminero: Caminero exited immediately after being hit in the left hand by a 97.6 mph sinker from Riley O’Brien while making his second plate appearance in the third inning. The Rays’ star third baseman underwent X-rays, which came back negative. He is day to day, according to the Rays.

DH Yandy Diaz: In his second All-Star Game, Díaz pinch-hit for starting DH Yordan Alvarez in the fifth inning, lost the grip on his bat on his first swing, then went down swinging on a changeup from Foster Griffin. He also struck out in his second at-bat. Go figure -- the AL’s leader in batting average has only had 10 multi-strikeout games for the Rays this season.

RHP Nick Martinez: Making his first All-Star Game appearance about three weeks shy of his 36th birthday, Martinez delivered the kind of quick, efficient and effective inning that’s made him such a big part of the Rays’ success this season. The right-hander retired Ozzie Albies on three pitches, struck out Brandon Marsh with a high fastball, then got Pages to ground out, with some help from Clement. Martinez often says he becomes a fan once the ball is put in play, and he definitely cheered on Clement for his inning-ending effort.

RHP Drew Rasmussen: In his second straight All-Star appearance, the Rays’ under-the-radar ace entered the game in the seventh inning, retired Matt Olson, then struck out Lopez on a nasty sweeper. Rasmussen then issued a rare walk to Stewart, at which point AL manager John Schneider lifted him in favor of Jacob Latz. Rasmussen appeared to thank Schneider on his way off the mound, and Schneider revealed the reason during an in-game interview on FOX: Rays manager Kevin Cash asked Schneider to pair up Rasmussen with Rutschman, his teammate at Oregon State, and the two got to work together again in the All-Star Game.

Red SoxLHP Aroldis Chapman: The hard-throwing left-hander made quick work of his latest All-Star Game appearance, throwing only six pitches to record the first two outs in the ninth. He struck out Home Run Derby champion Jordan Walker on a 97.6 mph sinker, got Olson to pop out on the first pitch, then gave way to Baker for the last out.

1B Willson Contreras: Fresh off his performance in the Home Run Derby, Contreras entered the game as a defensive replacement in the fourth inning. He smacked a 104.7 mph single off Raisel Iglesias in his only at-bat in the sixth inning, but he was promptly forced out at second on a grounder by Randy Arozarena. He was lifted for Murakami in the seventh.

OF Ceddanne Rafaela: A late addition to the AL All-Star team, Rafaela replaced Mike Trout in center field in the fourth inning and struck out on three pitches in his first at-bat against Dodgers lefty Justin Wrobleski in the seventh. He saw just one pitch and grounded out against Jhoan Duran in the ninth.

LHP Ranger Suarez: After being named an All-Star for the second time, Suarez wasn’t able to make a triumphant return to Philadelphia and instead remained in Boston to undergo treatment on the strained left groin that sent him to the 15-day IL last week.

YankeesOF Cody Bellinger: Batting with the bases loaded and two outs in the first inning, the AL’s starting right fielder -- back in the All-Star Game for the first time since 2019 -- whacked a two-run single to center field off Sánchez to start the scoring in the Midsummer Classic en route to earning MVP honors. The 101.1 mph single kept the inning alive for his teammate, Rice, to deliver another big hit.
OF Aaron Judge: Voted into the All-Star Game by fans for the eighth time, Judge didn’t make the trip to Philadelphia due to a stress fracture of his right rib.

1B Ben Rice: After falling short (but having a blast) in Monday night’s Home Run Derby, Rice came through for the AL in his first trip to the All-Star Game. After Bellinger’s two-run single put the AL squad on the board, Rice -- starting at first base after Guerrero declined to play -- smacked a 106.8 mph single to center off Sánchez to make it a 3-0 game. It’s the first time since 1962 (Roger Maris and Tom Tresh) that the Yankees had multiple players with at least one RBI in the Midsummer Classic.
RHP Cam Schlittler: The AL ERA leader decided not to pitch in his first All-Star Game but lined up with the rest of the Yankees’ contingent at Citizens Bank Park.

Guardians2B Travis Bazzana: Talk about a rookie season that’s been unforgettable, from making his debut with the Guardians on April 28 to now playing at the All-Star Game. Bazzana replaced Clement at second base in the sixth inning and went 0-for-2 at the plate, striking out in the seventh and grounding into a forceout to end the top of the ninth inning.

LHP Parker Messick: Messick took over for the second inning and pitched a 1-2-3 frame facing the NL’s 5-6-7 hitters. He got Max Muncy to pop out in foul territory, Ozzie Albies to ground out to shortstop and then struck out Brandon Marsh during a seven pitch at-bat, eventually freezing him on a 94.8 mph sinker for strike three.

RHP Cade Smith: “That’s disgusting,” Bryce Harper said, shaking his head after striking out on Smith’s splitter, the pitch that helped the right-hander to the first All-Star Game in his career. The Guardians’ closer threw a 12-pitch sixth inning that included striking out Harper and Corbin Carroll, who swung on a 99.4 mph fastball at the bottom of the zone.

RoyalsRHP Michael Wacha: Making his first All-Star appearance since 2015, Wacha was actually able to pitch in the game this time around and threw a quick, 1-2-3 third inning. He struck out Andy Pages swinging on a changeup, Drake Baldwin looking at a 95.9 mph sinker and then got Kyle Schwarber to ground out sharply to second base.

SS Bobby Witt Jr.: In his first All-Star start, Witt walked and scored in the first inning and beat out a close double-play ball in his second at-bat – so close that it required a long replay review that ultimately saw the safe call stand because of Witt’s blazing speed. Witt was mic’d up in the third inning as his teammate Wacha pitched and talked to the broadcast about his love of the game.

TigersC Dillon Dingler: The first Tigers catcher to be named an All-Star since Alex Avila in 2011, Dingler entered Tuesday’s game in the fourth to catch Joe Ryan and spent three scoreless innings behind the plate. Dingler only got one at-bat and struck out in the fifth against Foster Griffin.

OF Riley Greene: When he was named as a replacement starter for this year’s All-Star Game, Greene made some franchise history, too, becoming the first Tigers outfielder since Al Kaline to play in three consecutive All-Star Games. In this Midsummer Classic, Greene struck out twice.

SS Kevin McGonigle: A native of Philly suburb Drexel Hill, Pa., McGonigle grew up dreaming about going to an All-Star Game in Philadelphia as a fan. How about playing in one instead? McGonigle took over at shortstop in the bottom of the fifth inning and went 0-for-2 with a strikeout at the plate.

RHP Justin Verlander: Nursing hip and hamstring injuries, Verlander didn’t pitch Tuesday but soaked in every moment of his final All-Star Game as the AL Legend Pick by Commissioner Rob Manfred. The 43-year-old was mic’d up throughout the game.

TwinsOF Byron Buxton: An All-Star for the third time in five years, Buxton was elected as a starting outfielder for the AL, but he didn’t play because of a nagging hip injury. He still took in the All-Star festivities in Philadelphia, though.

RHP Joe Ryan: Pitching in his second consecutive All-Star Game, Ryan entered for the fourth inning Tuesday and pitched a clean frame. He allowed the AL’s first hit of the night when Juan Soto singled, but Ryan struck out the next two batters – Freddie Freeman and CJ Abrams – before getting Max Muncy to pop out to shortstop.

White Sox1B Munetaka Murakami: Murakami is doing it all in his first season in MLB. A day after participating in the Home Run Derby, the Japanese slugger entered the All-Star Game in the seventh inning to play first base. He struck out in his only at-bat, swinging at a 101.7 mph fastball from Mason Miller.

OF Tristan Peters: One of the best stories on a surging White Sox team full of great stories this year, Peters has had a whirlwind of a season so far. The former Savannah Banana and journeyman Minor Leaguer latched on with Chicago this year and played his way into a starting role. He found out he was going to the All-Star Game right after he hit for the cycle last Friday. Peters replaced Cody Bellinger in right field in the sixth on Tuesday and went 0-for-1 at the plate, striking out after challenging a pitch that just nicked the top of the strike zone in the eighth inning.

INF Miguel Vargas: Called on perhaps a little earlier than expected when he had to pinch-run for the injured Caminero in the third inning, Vargas announced his presence with a bang in the eighth with a Statcast-projected 433-foot home run off Justin Wrobleski as part of a 1-for-2 night. That allowed the baseball world to catch a glimpse of what Vargas has been doing all season in Chicago, blasting 21 home runs before the break.

AngelsOF Mike Trout: The 12-time All-Star received a huge ovation from his hometown crowd when introduced before the game, as he grew up in nearby Millville, N.J., and is a noted Philly sports fan. The three-time AL MVP earned the honor of leading off the game but struck out in his first at-bat. Trout, a two-time All-Star Game MVP, also grounded out softly to second base in the second inning against lefty Eduardo Rodriguez and hit a chopper to third to end the third, going 0-for-3 at the plate. But it was a memorable experience for Trout, who grew up 40 miles from the ballpark and spent time with his family.

AstrosDH Yordan Alvarez: The four-time All-Star headed into the game leading the AL with 31 homers, 111 hits and 70 RBIs and kept it going with a one-out single to spark the three-run rally in the opening frame. He also flied out to left against Eduardo Rodriguez to end the second, finishing 1-for-2 with a run scored. And while he’s a fearsome slugger at the plate, he has a softer side off the field, including bringing his 5-year-old son Jordan to the ballpark as much as possible.

Athletics1B Nick Kurtz: The slugger was elected as the starting first baseman for the AL but was placed on the 10-day IL on Saturday with a right thumb capsule strain and was held out of action. But Kurtz, a first-time All-Star and last year’s AL Rookie of the Year, made the trip to Philadelphia.

C Shea Langeliers: The first-time All-Star was mic'd up with batterymate Dylan Cease during the broadcast and helped guide him to strike out three of the four batters he faced in the first inning. He also walked in the first following an Alvarez single and lined a sharp single into center to lead off the third. The 28-year-old went 1-for-1 with a walk and is reaching his potential after being acquired in a trade with the Braves in 2022. He became the first Athletics catcher to be an All-Star since Terry Steinbach in 1989.

MarinersOF Randy Arozarena: An All-Star for the second straight season and the third time in his career, Arozarena came into the game as a defensive replacement in the fifth inning and grounded out to shortstop to end the sixth inning. He added a one-out single in the ninth to go 1-for-2. Arozarena is the 15th player in franchise history to be a multi-time All-Star and credited a change in his mentality at the plate for another strong year offensively.

RangersLHP Jacob Latz: With a tough lefty at the plate in the seventh in Luis Arraez, Latz was brought in with one on and two outs and struck Arraez out to end the inning. A first-time All-Star at 30 years old, Latz competed for a spot in the rotation in Spring Training but after losing out to Kumar Rocker, he worked his way into the closer role. Latz continued the club’s seven-year streak of sending a pitcher to the All-Star Game and has embraced his new role as a high-leverage reliever with a 1.61 ERA and 18 saves in 35 appearances.

Braves2B Ozzie Albies: Like his Braves teammates, a bemused Albies received some of the loudest boos from the Phillies' faithful during player introductions. The four-time All-Star started at second base and batted sixth, going hitless in two at-bats. In the second inning, he grounded out to short against Parker Messick. Albies grounded out to first to open the fifth against Nick Martinez.

C Drake Baldwin: First-time All-Star Baldwin caught Cristopher Sánchez, Eduardo Rodriguez and Riley O'Brien over the first three innings and hit ninth in the NL’s lineup. In his lone at-bat, he struck out on three pitches, taking a called third strike on Michael Wacha's 95.9 mph four-seamer, which clipped the outside corner for the second out in the third.

RHP Raisel Iglesias: Iglesias made the most of his long-awaited All-Star debut, notching a scoreless sixth inning on 14 pitches. Atlanta's closer induced a groundout to second from Kevin McGonigle and a lineout to left from Cody Bellinger. Following a single by Willson Contreras, Iglesias got Randy Arozarena to ground into a forceout to end the frame.

1B Matt Olson: How appropriate that Olson, who replaced Freddie Freeman in Atlanta, took over for him on defense in the fifth inning. The four-time All-Star flied out to left against Drew Rasmussen to lead off the seventh, then popped out to short against Aroldis Chapman in the ninth.

LHP Chris Sale: Although available to pitch, Sale did not take the mound in his 10th Midsummer Classic (second behind Mike Trout for most among active players) in front of a division rival’s home crowd.

MarlinsSS Otto Lopez: Lopez, who became the first Marlins shortstop named an All-Star since Hanley Ramirez in 2010, took over for CJ Abrams at short to open the sixth and settled into the cleanup spot. He struck out swinging on a sweeper from Drew Rasmussen to conclude a six-pitch at-bat in the seventh, then extended the game with a two-out single to right against Bryan Baker in the ninth.

RHP Max Meyer: The first-time All-Star wasn’t available to pitch as the Marlins monitor his workload after he had already surpassed his single-season career highs for innings and starts, but he took part in the All-Star festivities.

MetsOF Juan Soto: Batting second and starting in left field, Soto smirked as boos rained down during player introductions. He swung and missed on a 98 mph four-seamer from Dylan Cease in the first, offering an exasperated look as he walked back to the dugout. Soto later broke up the AL's no-hitter with a single up the middle against Joe Ryan to open the fourth. His second All-Star start and fifth appearance would come to a close when he was replaced on defense in the fifth.

NationalsSS CJ Abrams: Abrams, who joined Ernie Banks as the only shortstops to hit cleanup for the NL in the All-Star Game, chased Dylan Cease's slider to end the first inning and couldn't make contact on Joe Ryan's 95.5 mph four-seamer for the second out of the fourth. Abrams' second All-Star appearance came to an end when Otto Lopez took over for him on defense to begin the sixth inning.

OF James Wood: Back-to-back All-Star Wood replaced Brandon Marsh in right field in the sixth inning. He took a called third strike on Louis Varland's knuckle curve to cap a six-pitch at-bat to open the eighth.

LHP Foster Griffin: From three seasons in Japan to the Midsummer Classic, 30-year-old rookie Griffin proved he belongs by tossing a perfect fifth inning on 10 pitches. He fanned AL batting average leader Yandy Díaz and Dillon Dingler -- both on his changeup -- before getting Miguel Vargas to line out to center.

PhilliesRHP Jhoan Duran: NL manager Dave Roberts brought in first-time All-Star Duran with one out in the ninth, and he wasted no time flashing triple digits with a 100.3 mph offering on his first pitch. Though Duran allowed a single to Randy Arozarena, he induced consecutive groundouts to wrap up a four-pitch outing. Duran's appearance made the Phillies 6-for-6 in getting into the game.

1B Bryce Harper: There are few better ambassadors for the game than nine-time All-Star Harper, so moments before first pitch, Harper participated in an on-field interview with Ken Rosenthal explaining why Phillies fans are the "best fans in the world” and that “they show out for us every night." When Harper entered the game as a pinch-hitter for Schwarber in the sixth, he received a standing ovation from the hometown crowd. It was an eventful at-bat for a mic'd Harper, who asked for an ABS challenge -- for the sake of having one in the exhibition. On the next pitch, he chased Cade Smith's splitter for the strikeout, commenting, "That’s disgusting" on his walk back to the dugout.

LHP Jesús Luzardo: Added to the roster as a replacement, Luzardo showcased some of the All-Star Game’s best stuff. In his All-Star debut, Luzardo needed just six pitches to retire the AL in order in the fourth, striking out Riley Greene on a sweeper out of the zone and inducing groundouts to third from Ernie Clement and Mike Trout on consecutive pitches. His 98.9 mph sinker to Trout was the fastest pitch of the game at the time.

OF Brandon Marsh: Marsh, starting in right field and batting seventh in his first All-Star Game, saw 14 pitches between two at-bats, but struck out in both of them. He stared at Parker Messick's sinker to end the second inning, then swung through Nick Martinez's four-seamer in the fifth.

LHP Cristopher Sánchez: Two-time All-Star Sánchez became the first Phillies pitcher since Roy Halladay in 2011 and the seventh pitcher in Phillies history to start a Midsummer Classic, and he did so in front of the home crowd. Things began and ended promisingly, with Sánchez striking out Millville, N.J., native Mike Trout and Riley Greene on his trademark changeup. It's what happened in between that cost Sánchez three runs in a 34-pitch first inning, as he struggled to find a putaway offering and issued two walks. With two outs, Yankees teammates Cody Bellinger and Ben Rice produced back-to-back run-scoring singles up the middle against him.

DH Kyle Schwarber: Some of the biggest cheers of the night were for four-time All-Star Schwarber, who was the runner-up at Monday's Home Run Derby and led off for the NL Tuesday. He struck out on a nasty slider from Dylan Cease to cap a seven-pitch at-bat in the first, then experienced a "technology break" his next time up while mic'd. With help from catcher Shea Langeliers, Schwarber reinserted his earpiece and self-deprecatingly said: "Let’s see how this goes. Hopefully better than the first one." Schwarber did his best to get the broadcast booth a hit, but he sharply grounded out to second against Michael Wacha to end the third.

BrewersC William Contreras: Now a three-time All-Star, Contreras took over behind the plate for the NL in the fourth inning with Luzardo on the hill. The Brewers’ catcher flew out to right field against Smith, the Guardians’ dominant reliever, in the sixth.

RHP Jacob Misiorowski: The Brewers’ flamethrowing ace did not pitch in the All-Star Game, prioritizing rest ahead of an important second half for the NL Central leaders. On the red carpet before the Midsummer Classic, Misiorowski looked dapper in a three-piece suit that included a photo of his dog, Ember, stitched into the inner lining. During the second inning, the Brewers’ pitcher was mic’d up, along with Justin Verlander and Schlittler.

CardinalsRHP Riley O’Brien: In his first All-Star appearance, the Cardinals’ breakout closer handled the third inning for the NL. While O’Brien did not allow a run and ended with a strikeout of Rice, the right-hander was also part of an unfortunate moment. An up-and-in pitch to Caminero hit the Rays’ star on his left hand, forcing the slugger to immediately leave the game.

OF Jordan Walker: After taking down Schwarber to win the Home Run Derby -- the first Cardinals player to win the event – Walker made a late entrance in his first career All-Star Game. The Cards’ star outfielder took over in left field in the eighth and then stepped to the plate to lead off the bottom of the ninth, striking out against Chapman.

DH Iván Herrera: In the eighth inning, Herrera pinch-hit for Harper and grounded out in his lone at-bat. It was the first All-Star trip for the Cardinals’ catcher/DH after he was named to the roster as a replacement. This has been a breakout year for Herrera, who ranks seventh in the NL in on-base percentage (.381).

CubsOF Pete Crow-Armstrong: Chicago’s electric center fielder was mic’d up in the eighth inning of the All-Star Game and let out an, “Oh god,” as Vargas’ monster homer off Dodgers lefty Justin Wrobleski sailed out to left field. While Crow-Armstrong (making his second All-Star appearance) was the lone Cubs representative, the city of Chicago had four total with three on hand from the White Sox.

“That’s just good for the city -- it really is,” said Crow-Armstrong, who later delivered a single in the bottom of the eighth. “And they’re such a fun, young team. [White Sox manager Will] Venable is doing a killer job over there. And then you throw the Bears in the mix, Chicago’s in a really nice spot, sports wise.”

PiratesRHP Braxton Ashcraft: A first-time All-Star, Ashcraft did not appear in the All-Star Game due to pitching on Saturday for the Pirates and dealing with a blister issue. The righty heads into the second half with a 3.49 ERA and 128 strikeouts in 113 1/3 innings for Pittsburgh.

RHP Paul Skenes: The ace of the Pirates’ staff started for the NL in each of the previous two All-Star Games and became the fourth pitcher in MLB history to make an All-Star team in each of his first three seasons. Since he pitched on Sunday for Pittsburgh, however, Skenes was not available for the Midsummer Classic.
Skenes, Ashcraft show their lighter sides at All-Star Game

RedsRHP Chase Burns: The 23-year-old Burns is enjoying a breakout sophomore campaign (11-1, 2.54 ERA) with Cincinnati, but he was not available to pitch in the Midsummer Classic. Burns is dealing with tightness in his right groin and sat out his first All-Star appearance.

3B Sal Stewart: The Reds’ first-time All-Star replaced Max Muncy at third base in the fifth inning. In his first plate appearance, the 22-year-old Stewart worked a walk against Rasmussen in the seventh inning. Stewart had one more chance, but he grounded out to Baker for the final out of this year’s Midsummer Classic.

D-backsOF Corbin Carroll: This was Carroll’s second straight All-Star Game selection and the third in his first four full seasons in the big leagues. Entering the game as a defensive replacement in left field in the fifth, Carroll’s lone at-bat came in the sixth against Cade Smith, who struck him out swinging. Jordan Walker replaced him in left to start the eighth inning.

LHP Eduardo Rodriguez: Rodriguez was selected to his first All-Star Game in his 11th Major League season, and he made the most of his opportunity. Called on to pitch the second inning, Rodriguez made quick work of it. He got Ernie Clement to ground out to first on one pitch before getting Mike Trout to ground out to second and, finally, induced a Yordan Alvarez flyout to left. In all, it took Rodriguez just eight pitches to get through the inning.

Dodgers1B Freddie Freeman: In his 10th All-Star Game and sixth as the NL’s starting first baseman, Freeman had two plate appearances, drawing a walk off Dylan Cease in the first inning and striking out against Joe Ryan in the fourth. In the top of the first, Freeman, a Gold Glove Award winner, made a nice play to nab pitcher Christopher Sánchez’s off-target throw and got his foot back to the bag just before Junior Caminaro touched down. Freeman departed the game after four innings.

3B Max Muncy: This was Muncy’s third All-Star Game selection and his first as a starter. He logged four innings at third base and had a couple of at-bats. He popped out in the second inning against Parker Messick and again popped out in the fourth against Joe Ryan.

DH Shohei Ohtani: Ohtani was selected for his sixth consecutive All-Star Game, garnering the most votes (3,341,257) of any player during Phase I of the fan voting. However, irritation in his left knee caused Ohtani to miss the game and the festivities in Philadelphia. Ohtani was scheduled to have the knee drained after Sunday’s first-half finale and is expected to be back in the lineup Friday when the Dodgers open the second half against the Yankees.

OF Andy Pages: As a reward for a breakout year for the National League’s best team, Pages won the fan vote and got the start for the NL in center field, batting eighth. In his first at-bat, he struck out against Michael Wacha, and in his second and final at-bat of the game, he was the victim of robbery. Pages hit a grounder back up the middle that second baseman Ernie Clement backhanded and jumped to throw a strike to first, nailing Pages.

LHP Justin Wrobleski: Wrobleski was added to the NL roster as a replacement for the injured Chase Burns, and he could not have had a better ASG debut then he did when he came on in the seventh. Not known as a strikeout pitcher, Wrobleski fanned Travis Bazzana, Ceddanne Rafaela and Yandy Díaz in order. Sent out for the eighth, Wrobleski allowed a solo homer to Miguel Vargas. Per Sarah Langs, Wrobleski became the ninth different player to appear in an All-Star Game on their birthday. Billy Herman did it twice (1936 and ’37).

RHP Yoshinobu Yamamoto: Yamamoto was selected for the All-Star team for the second straight year and, like last year, was unavailable to pitch after starting Saturday against the Diamondbacks. Yamamoto did travel to Philadelphia for the All-Star festivities and looked sharp on the 2026 Red Carpet Show presented by Mastercard, wearing a black suit with a white shirt and a black crossover tie.

Giants2B Luis Arraez: In his first year with the Giants, Arraez was selected to his fourth All-Star Game. He entered the game in the top of the sixth and struck out swinging in his only at-bat against the Rangers’ Jacob Latz in the seventh. It was the only batter the left-handed Latz would face.

RHP Logan Webb: Webb was selected to his third consecutive All-Star Game, but after missing some time earlier this year due to injury, he was only going to pitch in the game in an emergency situation. He is scheduled to start during the Giants’ second-half-opening series against the Mariners.

PadresRHP Mason Miller: Miller, who is in the midst of one of the most dominant relief seasons in baseball history, made his second All-Star Game appearance -- and it was a short one. Brought in by NL manager Dave Roberts to start the ninth inning, Miller struck out Munetaka Murakami on four pitches, all of which were over 100 mph. He was taken out after that at-bat in order to get Phillies closer Jhoan Duran into the game.

RockiesC Hunter Goodman: Named to his second straight All-Star Game, Goodman took over behind the plate in the top of the seventh sporting a red, white and blue chest protector with the Liberty Bell on it. He was behind the dish when the Dodgers’ Justin Wrobleski struck out the side in the seventh. Goodman was aggressive in his lone plate appearance, swinging at the first pitch he saw from Louis Varland and lining out to short in the eighth.
items.titleZh: 蓝鸟助美联在2026全明星赛完封获胜
items.summaryZh: 蓝鸟投手Dylan Cease首局三振三人，Ernie Clement献精彩防守，Vladimir Guerrero Jr.缺阵。
bodyZh: 在庆祝美国250岁生日的全明星赛中，美国联盟投手大放异彩。周二在费城市民银行公园，美联投手群联手仅让国联击出三支安打，没有长打，并送出15次三振，以4-0完封对手。
在美联从2013年到2022年连续赢得九届全明星赛后，国联在过去三届中赢了两届。但本场比赛11位投手联手完封国联，是13年来全明星赛首次完封。
美联在第一局就取得领先，面对费城人先发投手Cristopher Sánchez攻下三分，洋基队友Cody Bellinger击出两分打点一垒安打，Ben Rice补上一分打点一垒安打，随后白袜的Miguel Vargas在第八局轰出阳春炮。Bellinger因打进比赛前两分获得MVP荣誉。
在一场投手主导的夜晚中，以下是2026年MLB全明星赛的逐个球员分析：
跳转至：美联东区 | 美联中区 | 美联西区 | 国联东区 | 国联中区 | 国联西区
蓝鸟 右投手Dylan Cease：蓝鸟史上第四位在全明星赛先发的投手，Cease在第一局用了25球，三振了Kyle Schwarber、Juan Soto和CJ Abrams，只让Freddie Freeman保送上垒。球迷们得以通过麦克风听到Cease与捕手Shea Langeliers讨论配球过程。
二垒手Ernie Clement：美联得票最多的球员，在第九棒两次无安打，但他在二垒上演了一次精彩防守——对手正是去年世界大赛第七场对他完成防守美技的道奇球员。第五局两出局无人上垒时，Clement迅速移动到中路拦截Andy Pages的滚地球，然后强力跳起传球刺杀了Pages，后者（可以理解）似乎以为他能从光芒右投手Nick Martinez手中击出安打。
一垒手Vladimir Guerrero Jr.：尽管上半季表现平平，他仍被票选进入第六次全明星赛先发，但Guerrero拒绝出场，回到佛罗里达州坦帕市休养酸痛的背部。
右投手Louis Varland：在第八局下半登板，Varland状态火热。他的第一球是100.2英里/小时的伸卡球，面对国民重炮James Wood，虽然是坏球。Varland让Wood看着曲球三振，然后被Pete Crow-Armstrong打出安打，随后再抓下两个出局数，完成11球的一局。
金莺 捕手Adley Rutschman：或许正合适，Rutschman在他职业生涯防守最好的赛季中，于第七局作为防守替补进入他的第三次全明星赛。这位金莺明星捕手接了一个无安打的一局，在第八局率先击出滚地球出局，然后在本垒板后完成了美联的联合完封。他还经历了两次投捕重聚：一次是与前俄勒冈州立大学队友Drew Rasmussen，另一次是与前金莺后援投手Bryan Baker。
光芒 右投手Bryan Baker：光芒最新投手成功故事从美联牛棚被叫上来抓第九局最后一个出局数，他做到了。在让Otto Lopez打出安打后，Baker让Sal Stewart击出软弱的投手方向滚地球，他干净利落地接球传向一垒的村上宗隆，锁定了美联完封。然后他与前金莺队友Rutschman一起庆祝。
三垒手Junior Caminero：Caminero在第三次上场打击时，被Riley O’Brien的97.6英里/小时伸卡球击中左手，随即退场。这位光芒明星三垒手接受了X光检查，结果为阴性。据光芒队消息，他每日观察。
指定打击Yandy Díaz：在他的第二次全明星赛中，Díaz在第五局代打先发指定打击Yordan Alvarez，第一次挥棒时球棒脱手，然后被Foster Griffin的变速球三振。他第二次打击也遭三振。想想看——美联打击率领先者本赛季为光芒队仅有10场比赛单场多次三振。
右投手Nick Martinez：在36岁生日前三周左右首次亮相全明星赛，Martinez投出了快速、高效且有效的一局，这正是他本赛季为光芒成功的重要组成部分。这位右投手用三球解决Ozzie Albies，用高速四缝线快速球三振Brandon Marsh，然后在Clement的帮助下让Pages击出滚地球出局。Martinez常说一旦球被打进场他就变成球迷，他确实为Clement的局末表现欢呼。

右投手Drew Rasmussen：连续第二次入选全明星赛，这位光芒低调王牌在第七局登场，解决了Matt Olson，然后用一颗恶心的滑曲球三振Lopez。随后Rasmussen罕见地保送了Stewart，此时美联教练John Schneider将他换下，由Jacob Latz接手。Rasmussen在走下投手丘时似乎向Schneider道谢，Schneider在FOX的采访中透露了原因：光芒教练Kevin Cash要求他将Rasmussen与他在俄勒冈州立大学的队友Rutschman配对，两人再次在全明星赛中并肩作战。
红袜 左投手Aroldis Chapman：这位刚腕左投手在他的最新全明星赛中速战速决，只用了六球便取得第九局前两个出局数。他以97.6英里/小时的伸卡球三振了全垒打大赛冠军Jordan Walker，让Olson第一球就击出内野高飞球，然后由Baker上场抓最后一个出局数。
一垒手Willson Contreras：刚参加完全垒打大赛，Contreras在第四局作为防守替补登场。他在第六局唯一一次打击中，从Raisel Iglesias手中击出104.7英里/小时的安打，但随即被Randy Arozarena的滚地球封杀在二垒。他在第七局被村上宗隆替换。
外野手Ceddanne Rafaela：作为美联全明星队的后期增补，Rafaela在第四局取代Mike Trout镇守中外野，并在第七局面对道奇左投Justin Wrobleski的第一次打击中三球三振。他在第九局看见一球，被Jhoan Duran解决出局。
左投手Ranger Suarez：在第二次入选全明星赛后，Suarez未能重返费城荣耀，而是留在波士顿接受左腹股沟拉伤的治疗，这让他上周进入了15天伤病名单。
洋基 外野手Cody Bellinger：在第一局两出局满垒时，美联先发右外野手——自2019年后首次重返全明星赛——从中路击出一支两分打点一垒安打，为仲夏经典赛首开纪录，并最终获得MVP荣誉。这颗101.1英里/小时的安打延续了攻势，让他的队友Rice得以击出另一支关键安打。
外野手Aaron Judge：第八次被球迷票选进入全明星赛，但Judge因右肋骨应力性骨折没有前往费城。

一垒手Ben Rice：在周一晚上的全垒打大赛中失利（但玩得很开心）后，Rice在他的首次全明星赛中为美联建功。在Bellinger的两分打点安打为美联得分后，Rice——在Guerrero拒绝出战后先发一垒——从Sánchez手中击出106.8英里/小时的安打，将比分改写为3-0。这是自1962年（Roger Maris和Tom Tresh）以来洋基首次有多名球员在仲夏经典赛中各有至少一分打点。
右投手Cam Schlittler：美联自责分率领先者决定不在他的第一次全明星赛中投球，但他与其他洋基成员一起在市民银行公园列队。
守护者 二垒手Travis Bazzana：说到一个难忘的新秀赛季，从4月28日在守护者队首秀，到现在参加全明星赛。Bazzana在第六局取代Clement担任二垒手，打击上2打数0安打，第七局遭三振，第九局上半击出滚地球造成封杀出局。
左投手Parker Messick：Messick在第二局接替，面对国联的第5、6、7棒投出三上三下。他让Max Muncy击出界外高飞球被接杀，Ozzie Albies游击方向滚地球出局，然后三振了Brandon Marsh，后者在七球打席中最后被一颗94.8英里/小时的伸卡球冻结三振。
右投手Cade Smith：“真恶心，” Bryce Harper在挥空Smith的指叉球后摇着头说，这颗球帮助这位右投手生涯首次入选全明星赛。守护者终结者投了12球的第六局，包括三振Harper和Corbin Carroll，后者挥棒击中了好球带下方的99.4英里/小时快速球。
皇家 右投手Michael Wacha：自2015年以来首次入选全明星赛，Wacha这次真的能上场投球，并投出一个快速的三上三下第三局。他让Andy Pages看着变速球挥空三振，Drake Baldwin看着95.9英里/小时的伸卡球三振，然后让Kyle Schwarber击出强劲的二垒方向滚地球出局。
游击手Bobby Witt Jr.：在他的首次全明星先发中，Witt在第一局保送上垒并得分，第二次打击中他成功跑上一垒——由于他飞快的速度，需要长时间回放审查才确认安全上垒。Witt在第三局队友Wacha投球时佩戴麦克风，向转播谈论他对比赛的热爱。
老虎 捕手Dillon Dingler：自2011年Alex Avila以来首位入选全明星赛的老虎捕手，Dingler在周二第四局上场接替Joe Ryan，在本垒板后蹲捕三局无失分。Dingler只有一次打击，在第五局被Foster Griffin三振。
外野手Riley Greene：当他被任命为本届全明星赛的替补先发时，Greene也创造了队史，成为自Al Kaline以来首位连续三届参加全明星赛的老虎外野手。在这次仲夏经典赛中，Greene两次遭三振。
游击手Kevin McGonigle：费城郊区德雷克塞尔希尔（宾夕法尼亚州）本地人，McGonigle成长过程中梦想作为球迷去费城看全明星赛。但亲自上场参赛呢？McGonigle在第五局下半接替游击手，打击上2打数0安打，一次三振。
右投手Justin Verlander：因臀部和大腿伤势，Verlander周二没有投球，但他作为由专员Rob Manfred指定的美联传奇人选，沉浸在他最后一次全明星赛的每一刻。这位43岁老将在整场比赛中佩戴麦克风。
双城 外野手Byron Buxton：五年内第三次入选全明星赛，Buxton被选为美联先发外野手，但因持续困扰的臀部伤势没有上场。不过他仍然参加了费城的全明星庆祝活动。
右投手Joe Ryan：连续第二年参加全明星赛，Ryan在周二第四局登场，投出干净的一局。他被Juan Soto击出美联当晚第一支安打，但随后三振了接下来两名打者——Freddie Freeman和CJ Abrams，然后让Max Muncy击出游击方向高飞球出局。
白袜 一垒手村上宗隆（Munetaka Murakami）：村上在MLB的第一个赛季大显身手。在全垒打大赛参赛后的第二天，这位日本重炮在第七局进入全明星赛担任一垒手。他唯一一次打击被Mason Miller的101.7英里/小时快速球挥空三振。
外野手Tristan Peters：在白袜这支本赛季充满故事的崛起的球队中，Peters是最好故事之一，他的赛季迄今为止如旋风般精彩。前萨凡纳香蕉队球员和游走小联盟的浪人，今年加入芝加哥并争取到先发角色。上周五他刚完成完全打击后就得知自己将参加全明星赛。Peters在第六局取代Cody Bellinger担任右外野手，打击上1打数0安打，在第八局挑战一颗擦到好球带顶端的球后遭三振。
内野手Miguel Vargas：也许比预期更早被召唤——他在第三局为受伤的Caminero代跑——Vargas在第八局用一颗Statcast测算433英尺的全垒打宣告自己的存在，面对Justin Wrobleski，全场2打数1安打。这让棒球界得以一窥Vargas本赛季在芝加哥的表现：全明星赛前轰出21支全垒打。

天使 外野手Mike Trout：12次全明星赛的Trout在赛前介绍时受到家乡球迷的热烈欢呼，他在附近的新泽西州米尔维尔长大，是知名的费城体育迷。这位三届美联MVP获得率先上场的荣誉，但第一次打击被三振。两届全明星赛MVP的Trout在第二局面对左投Eduardo Rodriguez时击出软弱二垒方向滚地球，第三局则打出三垒方向弹跳球，全场3打数0安打。但这对Trout来说是一次难忘的经历，他在距离球场40英里外长大，与家人共度了时光。
太空人 指定打击Yordan Alvarez：这位四届全明星赛球员入场时领跑美联，有31支全垒打、111支安打和70分打点，并在首局一出局后击出安打，点燃三分攻势。他还面对Eduardo Rodriguez在第二局击出左外野高飞球被接杀，全场2打数1安打，跑回一分。虽然他在打击区上是可怕的重炮手，场下却有温柔的一面，包括尽可能把5岁的儿子Jordan带到球场。
运动家 一垒手Nick Kurtz：这位重炮手被票选为美联先发一垒手，但周六因右手拇指关节囊扭伤被放入10天伤病名单，无法上场。但首次入选全明星赛的Kurtz、去年的美联最佳新人，仍前往费城。

捕手Shea Langeliers：首次入选全明星赛的Langeliers在转播中与投球搭档Dylan Cease一起佩戴麦克风，帮助引导他在第一局四名打者中三振三人。他还在第一局Alvarez安打后保送上垒，并在第三局率先击出中路平飞安打。这位28岁球员1打数1安打，一次保送，在2022年通过交易从勇士队获得后正在发挥潜力。他成为自1989年Terry Steinbach以来首位入选全明星赛的运动家捕手。
水手 外野手Randy Arozarena：连续第二个赛季、生涯第三次入选全明星赛，Arozarena在第五局作为防守替补上场，并在第六局击出游击方向滚地球出局。他在第九局加一支一出局安打，全场2打数1安打。Arozarena是队史第15位多次入选全明星赛的球员，他将另一个强劲的进攻赛季归功于打击心态的改变。

游骑兵 左投手Jacob Latz：第七局面对左打Luis Arraez的棘手局面，Latz在一人出局、一人在垒的情况下被换上场，三振了Arraez结束该局。30岁的Latz首次入选全明星赛，他在春训中竞争轮值位置，但输给Kumar Rocker后，转而担任终结者。Latz延续了球队连续七年有投手入选全明星赛的纪录，他欣然接受了新角色——高压力后援投手，自责分率1.61，35次出场中18次救援成功。
勇士 二垒手Ozzie Albies：像他的勇士队友们一样，Albie在球员介绍时受到费城球迷最响亮的嘘声。这位四届全明星赛球员先发二垒，打第六棒，两次打击无安打。第二局他对Parker Messick击出游击方向滚地球出局。第五局开打时他对Nick Martinez击出一垒方向滚地球出局。
捕手Drake Baldwin：首次入选全明星赛的Baldwin在前三局接捕Cristopher Sánchez、Eduardo Rodriguez和Riley O'Brien，在国联打击顺序中打第九棒。他唯一一次打击是三球三振，面对Michael Wacha的95.9英里/小时四缝线快速球，第三球被判好球，成为第三局第二个出局数。
右投手Raisel Iglesias：Iglesias在期待已久的全明星首秀中表现亮眼，14球投出无失分的第六局。勇士终结者让Kevin McGonigle击出二垒方向滚地球，Cody Bellinger击出左外野高飞球被接杀。在Willson Contreras击出安打后，Iglesias让Randy Arozarena击出滚地球造成封杀，结束该局。
一垒手Matt Olson：在亚特兰大接替Freddie Freeman的Olson，在第五局防守时也接替了他，这再合适不过了。这位四届全明星赛球员在第七局对Drew Rasmussen击出左外野高飞球，第九局对Aroldis Chapman击出游击方向高飞球出局。
左投手Chris Sale：尽管可以上场投球，但Sale没有在分区对手主场观众面前登上投手丘，这是他第10次仲夏经典赛（现役球员中仅次于Mike Trout）。
马林鱼 游击手Otto Lopez：Lopez成为自2010年Hanley Ramirez以来首位入选全明星赛的马林鱼游击手，他在第六局接替CJ Abrams守游击，并承担第四棒任务。他在第七局六球打席中被Drew Rasmussen的滑曲球挥空三振，然后在第九局从Bryan Baker手中击出两出局右外野安打，延续了比赛。
右投手Max Meyer：首次入选全明星赛的Meyer无法上场投球，因为马林鱼在监控他的投球工作量——他已经超过了单赛季职业生涯最高的局数和先发次数，但他参加了全明星庆祝活动。
大都会 外野手Juan Soto：担任第二棒、先发左外野的Soto在球员介绍时听到嘘声一片，他露出苦笑。第一局他对Dylan Cease的98英里/小时四缝线快速球挥棒落空，走回休息区时一脸无奈。Soto后来在第四局从Joe Ryan手中击出中路安打，打破了美联的无安打比赛。他的第二次全明星先发和第五次亮相在第五局防守被替换后结束。
国民 游击手CJ Abrams：Abrams与Ernie Banks一样是唯一在全明星赛中国联打第四棒的游击手，他在第一局追打Dylan Cease的滑球三振出局，第四局未能碰到Joe Ryan的95.5英里/小时四缝线快速球，成为该局第二个出局。Abrams的第二次全明星赛在第六局Otto Lopez接替其防守后结束。
外野手James Wood：连续入选全明星赛的Wood在第六局接替Brandon Marsh镇守右外野。他在第八局六球打席中被Louis Varland的指关节曲球三振出局。
左投手Foster Griffin：从在日本打三年球到仲夏经典赛，30岁的新秀Griffin用10球投出完美的第五局，证明自己属于这里。他让美联打击率领先者Yandy Díaz和Dillon Dingler挥空三振——均是用变速球——然后让Miguel Vargas击出中外野高飞球被接杀。
费城人 右投手Jhoan Duran：国联教练Dave Roberts在第九局一出局时派上首次入选全明星赛的Duran，他毫不浪费时间，第一球就飙出100.3英里/小时。尽管Duran让Randy Arozarena打出安打，但他连续制造两个滚地球出局，完成四球的出场。Duran的出场使费城人队所有六名入选球员都上了场。

一垒手Bryce Harper：九届全明星赛球员Harper是比赛最好的大使之一，因此在开赛前，Harper参加了Ken Rosenthal的场内采访，解释为什么费城球迷是“世界上最好的球迷”，而且“他们每晚都为我们挺身而出”。当Harper在第六局代打Schwarber时，他受到主场观众起立鼓掌。对于佩戴麦克风的Harper来说，这是一个有事件的一打席，他要求进行一次ABS挑战——仅仅是为了在表演赛中尝试一下。下一球，他追打Cade Smith的指叉球遭到三振，走回休息区时评论道：“真恶心。”
左投手Jesús Luzardo：作为替补加入名单的Luzardo展示了他全明星赛中最好的球种之一。在全明星首秀中，Luzardo仅用六球就让美联在第四局三上三下，用一颗滑曲球三振了Riley Greene，并连续让Ernie Clement和Mike Trout击出三垒方向滚地球。他对Trout投出的98.9英里/小时伸卡球是当时比赛中最快的球。
外野手Brandon Marsh：Marsh在他的首次全明星赛中先发右外野，打第七棒，两次打席共看到14球，但均遭三振。他目送Parker Messick的伸卡球结束第二局，然后在第五局挥空Nick Martinez的四缝线快速球。

左投手Cristopher Sánchez：两届全明星赛球员Sánchez成为自2011年Roy Halladay以来首位代表费城人在仲夏经典赛先发的投手，也是队史第七位，他在主场观众面前做到了。开局和结尾都很有希望，Sánchez用他标志性的变速球三振了米尔维尔（新泽西州）本地人Mike Trout和Riley Greene。但在中间发生的事情让Sánchez在34球的第一局中丢掉三分，他难以找到解决打者的球种，并送出两次保送。两出局后，洋基队友Cody Bellinger和Ben Rice连续从中路击出打点安打。

指定打击Kyle Schwarber：当晚最大的欢呼声中有一部分献给了四届全明星赛球员Schwarber，他是周一全垒打大赛的亚军，周二为国联打第一棒。他在第一局被Dylan Cease的恶心滑球三振，结束七球打席，然后下一次上场时，佩戴麦克风的他经历了“技术暂停”。在捕手Shea Langeliers的帮助下，Schwarber重新插入耳机，自嘲地说：“看看这次怎么样。希望比第一次好。”Schwarber尽力为转播席制造安打，但他在第三局被Michael Wacha的滚地球直接解决，结束该局。
酿酒人 捕手William Contreras：如今是三届全明星赛球员，Contreras在第四局与Luzardo搭档，接替国联的捕手位置。这位酿酒人捕手在第六局对守护者强力后援投手Smith击出右外野高飞球被接杀。
右投手Jacob Misiorowski：酿酒人的火球王牌没有在全明星赛中投球，优先在国联中区领头羊重要的下半程前休息。在仲夏经典赛前的红毯上，Misiorowski身着三件套西装，内衬缝有他的狗Ember的照片，看起来十分帅气。在第二局，这位酿酒人投手与Justin Verlander和Schlittler一起佩戴麦克风。
红雀 右投手Riley O’Brien：在他的首次全明星赛中，红雀的突破性终结者负责国联第三局。尽管O’Brien没有失分，并以三振Rice结束该局，但这位右投手也参与了一个不幸的时刻。一个上到内侧的球击中了Caminero的左手，迫使这位重炮手立即退赛。
外野手Jordan Walker：在击败Schwarber赢得全垒打大赛——红雀队史首位夺冠球员后，Walker在他的首次全明星赛中较晚登场。这位红雀明星外野手在第八局接替左外野，然后第九局首棒上场打击，被Chapman三振。
指定打击Iván Herrera：在第八局，Herrera代打Harper，并在唯一一次打击中击出滚地球出局。这是他首次全明星之旅，在被选为替补后入选。对Herrera来说这是突破性的一年，他的上垒率（.381）在国联排名第七。
小熊 外野手Pete Crow-Armstrong：芝加哥的电动中外野手在全明星赛第八局佩戴麦克风，当Vargas的怪物全垒打飞出左外野时，他发出了“哦，天哪”的感叹。尽管Crow-Armstrong（第二次入选全明星赛）是小熊队唯一代表，但芝加哥市共有四名球员，白袜队有三人在场。
“这对这座城市是好事——确实如此。”Crow-Armstrong说，他随后在第八局下半击出安打。“他们是一支有趣、年轻的球队。[白袜教练Will] Venable在那里做得非常棒。再加上熊队，芝加哥在体育方面处境很好。”
海盗 右投手Braxton Ashcraft：首次入选全明星赛的Ashcraft因周六为海盗队先发并处理水泡问题而未在全明星赛中登场。这位右投手上半季自责分率3.49，在113又1/3局中送出128次三振。
右投手Paul Skenes：海盗王牌在过去两届全明星赛中均为国联先发，成为MLB历史上第四位在生涯前三个赛季都入选全明星队的投手。但由于周日为匹兹堡先发，Skenes无法参加仲夏经典赛。
Skenes与Ashcraft在全明星赛展现轻松一面
红人 右投手Chase Burns：23岁的Burns在辛辛那提迎来突破性的二年级赛季（11胜1负，自责分率2.54），但他无法在仲夏经典赛中投球。Burns正处理右腹股沟紧绷的问题，缺席了他的首次全明星赛。
三垒手Sal Stewart：红人首次入选全明星赛的Stewart在第五局取代Max Muncy担任三垒手。在他的首次打席中，22岁的Stewart在第七局从Rasmussen手中选到保送。Stewart还有一次机会，但他对Baker击出滚地球成为本届仲夏经典赛最后一个出局。
响尾蛇 外野手Corbin Carroll：这是Carroll连续第二次入选全明星赛，也是他大联盟前四个完整赛季中的第三次。他在第五局作为防守替补进入左外野，唯一的打席在第六局面对Cade Smith，被三振出局。Jordan Walker在第八局开始前接替他守左外野。
左投手Eduardo Rodriguez：Rodriguez在他第11个大联盟赛季首次入选全明星赛，并充分利用了这次机会。第二局被叫上场后，他迅速解决该局。他第一球就让Ernie Clement击出一垒方向滚地球出局，然后让Mike Trout击出二垒方向滚地球，最后让Yordan Alvarez击出左外野高飞球被接杀。总共只用了八球。

道奇 一垒手Freddie Freeman：在他的第10场全明星赛、第六次作为国联先发一垒手中，Freeman有两次打席：第一局从Dylan Cease选到保送，第四局被Joe Ryan三振。在第一局上半，金手套奖得主Freeman做出漂亮防守，接住了投手Christopher Sánchez的偏离目标传向一垒的球，并在Junior Caminaro触垒前将脚踩回垒包。Freeman在四局后离场。
三垒手Max Muncy：这是Muncy第三次入选全明星赛，也是首次作为先发。他守了三局三垒，有几次打席。第二局对Parker Messick击出高飞球被接杀，第四局对Joe Ryan再次击出高飞球被接杀。
指定打击大谷翔平（Shohei Ohtani）：大谷连续第六年入选全明星赛，在第一阶段球迷投票中获得最多票（3,341,257票）。然而，左膝发炎使大谷错过了比赛和费城的庆祝活动。原计划在周日上半季收官战后抽掉膝盖积水，预计周五道奇对阵洋基的下半季开打战中回归阵容。
外野手Andy Pages：作为对国联最佳球队突破性一年的奖励，Pages赢得球迷投票，为国联先发镇守中外野，打第八棒。在首次打击中，他被Michael Wacha三振；在第二次也是最后一次打席中，他成为防守美技的受害者。Pages击出中间方向的滚地球，二垒手Ernie Clement反手接球后跳起传向一垒，刺杀了Pages。

左投手Justin Wrobleski：Wrobleski作为受伤的Chase Burns的替补加入国联名单，他在第七局上场时迎来了不可能更好的全明星赛首秀。不以三振闻名的Wrobleski连续三振了Travis Bazzana、Ceddanne Rafaela和Yandy Díaz。第八局再上场时，他让Miguel Vargas击出阳春全垒打。据Sarah Langs统计，Wrobleski成为第九位在生日当天出场参加全明星赛的球员。Billy Herman曾两次做到（1936年和1937年）。
右投手山本由伸（Yoshinobu Yamamoto）：山本连续第二年入选全明星队，与去年一样，他在周六对响尾蛇先发后无法上场投球。山本确实前往费城参加全明星庆祝活动，在由Mastercard呈现的2026红毯秀上，他身着黑色西装、白衬衫和黑色交叉领带，显得十分帅气。
巨人 二垒手Luis Arraez：在巨人的第一年，Arraez第四次入选全明星赛。他在第六局上半进入比赛，唯一的打席在第七局对游骑兵的Jacob Latz挥空三振。这是左投Latz唯一面对的打者。
右投手Logan Webb：Webb连续第三年入选全明星赛，但今年因伤缺席了一些时间后，他只在紧急情况下才会投球。他计划在巨人下半季首系列对水手的比赛中先发。
教士 右投手Mason Miller：Miller正处于棒球史上最具统治力的后援赛季之一，他第二次亮相全明星赛——而且很短暂。国联教练Dave Roberts派他在第九局开局投球，Miller用四球三振了村上宗隆，全部球速超过100英里/小时。他随后被换下，让费城人终结者Jhoan Duran上场。
落矶 捕手Hunter Goodman：连续第二年入选全明星赛的Goodman在第七局上半接替捕手，佩戴着一件印有自由钟的红、白、蓝护胸。他在道奇队Justin Wrobleski第七局三振三连珠时在本垒板后。Goodman在唯一的打席中十分激进，他对Louis Varland的第一球就挥棒，击出游击方向平飞球被接杀。
